volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOLUME XIX
THE CHOLAS
INSCRIPTIONS OF PARAKESARIVARMAN

inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1904.)
MĒL-SĒVŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VṚISHABHAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete piece recording a gift of gold for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple, by Pirāntaka Pallavaraiyaṉ Ariñjigai. The name of the donor
suggests that he was probably an officer under Ariñjaya son of Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1907.)
TIRUVIŚALŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ŚIVAYŌGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep left in charge of one Brahmādirāyar of
Peruveṅgūr, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruviśalūr-Perumānaḍigaḷ
at Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a dēvadāna and brahmadēya on the
northern bank of the river. The donor's name is lost. Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam, which must have been named after a title of Parāntaka I (or
Pallava Nandivarman III) included in itself besides Tiruviśalūr, the village Tiruk-
kaḷittaṭṭai also (See No. 5 below). This might be an inscription of Āditya II Karikāla.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1907.)
TIRUVIḌAIMARUDŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAHĀLIṄGASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a sword with its hilt made of sandal-wood (śrīkhaṇḍam)
evidently as an offering to the god, by Mutti Tirunā[raṇaṉ] of the Śiṅgaḷāntaka-terinda-
Kaikkōḷar (a regiment named after Siṅgaḷāntaka i.e. Parāntaka I). This may be an
inscription of Ariñjaya.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1907.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records the construction of a window, a door, the doorpost and steps in front
of the maṇḍapa of the temple of Mūlasthānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tiruviḍaimarudil,
by the regiment called the Kaikkōḷa-perumbaḍai. This gift was named after, and
hence placed under the protection of, Tigai (Diśai) Āyirattaiññūṟṟuvar, who are stated to
be their Āchchamār (spiritual head ?). The writing resembles that of No. 254 of 1907
which belongs to Rājarāja I, and consequently the Parakēsari of this record may be
equated with his son Rājēndra-Chōḷa I.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] kopparakecaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 2 āvatu tiruviṭaimarutil

l.ta: pil tiruppalakaṇiyum tirukkatavum nilaiyum paṭiyum kaikkoḷapperumpaṭai-

edition.l: inta dharmma[t*]tiṉai rakṣittār śrīpātam eṅkaḷ sirattiṉ ||—

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 296 of 1908.)
TIRUKKAḶITTAṬṬAI, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VĒDAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of one eighth (of a vēli ?) of land to the west of the
sacred tank, for burning a lamp in the temple of Tirukkuḍittiṭṭai-Perumāḷ at Amaṉi
nārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam on the northern bank of the river, by Perumāṉ
Pōṟṟi wife of a certain Neṟkuppai-uḍaiyāṉ. This is possibly an inscription of
Āditya II Karikāla son of Sundara-Chōḷa Parāntaka II.

edition.l:
maṇavāṭṭi perumāṉ poṟṟi cantr[ā]ticcavaṟ erikka vaittatu tirukkuḷattiṉ me(l)lai
araikkāl itu pa[nm]āhēśvararakṣai |||—

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1911.)
KUMBHAKŌṆAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, NĀGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This registers a sale of a quarter (vēli) of land by the mūlaparishat of Tiruk-
kuḍamukkil a dēvadāna in Pāmbūr-nāḍu on the northern bank of the river, to
bēhalf of the temple of Tirukkīḻkōṭṭattu-Perumāḷ) for 25 kaḻañju of gold which had
been endowed for a perpetual lamp in the temple by a certain Paḍaivaḷḷuvaṉ
Kāḍaṉ Āchchaṉ. This land is said to have been presented to the assembly as
abhishēka-dakshiṇai (evidently by the king) on the occasion of his coronation.
As the land endowed by this Kāḍaṉ Āchchaṉ is referred to in a record of Āditya
II from the same place (A.R. No. 225 of 1911., S.I.I. Vol. III, No. 204), the
present inscription may be assigned to the same king.

l.ta: yuḷ ḷakappaṭṭa nilam va ikkāṟ c[e]-

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1911.)
ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Tirukkīḻkōṭṭattu-Paramasvāmi at Tirukkuḍamukkil, a dēvadāna in Pāmbūr-
nāḍu on the northern bank of the river, by Dēvaṉ Rājādittaṉ (a member) of the
Dānatoṅga-teriñja-Kaikkōḷar (regiment), with 7(1/2) nāḻi of ghee supplied every month
by two shepherds who received the sheep. Dānatuṅga was a title of king Parāntaka I
(M.E.R. 1918, Part II, para 22). This is probably a record of Ariñjaya.

l.ta: m ivviruvom pāti[pp]āti ney tiṅkaḷ oroto

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1911.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep by one Kaḍikāvaṉ Kaḷḷāṉ of the Vīraśōḻa-teriñja-
Kaikkōḷar (regiment) for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirukkaḻ
kōṭṭattu-Paramasvāmi at Tirukkuḍamukkil. ‘Vīraśōḻa’ was a title of Parāntaka I.
This may be a record of Ariñjaya.

l.ta: ṉum caṅkaṉ mātevaṉum vacam vaitta vā-

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1911.)
TILLAISTHĀNAM, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, GHṚITASTHĀNĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription has been surmised to belong to Uttama-Chōḷa. It registers
a gift of land for burning a lamp in the temple at Tiruneyttānam by one Koṟṟaṉ
Arumoḻi alias Vānavaṉ Pēraraiyaṉ of Āṟṟūr in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu after purchasing the
same for 25 kaḻañju of gold. Mention is also made of a land which had been
presented to the same temple by Naṅgai Varaguṇa-Perumānār, wife of the Koḍumbāḷūr
chief Parāntaka Iḷaṅgōvēḷār.

edition.p:
Published in South Indian Inscriptions,
Vol. III, No. 127.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1920.)
UḌAIYĀRKUḌI NEAR KĀṬṬUMANNĀRKŌYIL, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ANANTĒŚVARASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This might be an inscription of either Ariñjaya or Āditya II, as the date of the
gift is given as year 2, Siṁha, uvā (amāvāsya), Monday, Maghā, which gives the equi-
valent A.D. 958, August 16, f.d.t. 65, f.d.n. ·07. Another but less probable equivalent
(for Uttama-Chōḷa) is A.D. 968, July 27. This records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of
gold for (the purchase of) 96 sheep and a ram towards the maintenance of a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruvanantēśvarattāḻvār at Śrī-Vīranārāyaṇa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya on the northern bank (of the river), by Nakkaṉ
Baṭṭālaki a songstress attached to the periya-vēḷam (royal palace).

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 540 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep and a ram for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tiruvanantēśvarattāḻvār at Vīranārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, by
Parāntakaṉ-Mādēvaḍigaḷ alias Śembiyaṉ-Mādēviyār the daughter of Maḻavaraiyar and
queen of Gaṇḍarādityadēvar ‘who was pleased to go west’, i.e., was deceased. This is
evidently an inscription of Ariñjaya since Gaṇḍarāditya's demise seems to be implied
as a recent event.

edition.l: vārkku vaiytta nōntāviḷakku 1- kku vaitta vāṭu 96 takar 1 [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is also probably an inscription of Ariñjaya. It records a gift of
12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple, by a servant-
woman (of the royal palace) named Dīraṉ Tiṟ[ṟa]li.

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp with
an uḻakku of ghee every day in the temple, by Dēśavalavaṉ Sūryyaṉ of Vāṉavamaṅ-
galam in Sindhavāy-nāḍu, a member of the Kōdaṇḍarāma-teriñja-Kaikkōḷar regiment,
which was named probably after a surname of Rājāditya. Like No. 10 above this
may also be assigned either to Ariñjaya or Āditya II.

l.ta: ṇṭu nicatam uḻakku ney aṭṭuvatāno maṉṟā-

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold deposited with some shepherds,
a lamp in the same temple, by Aiyyāṟaṉ Śēndaṉ alias Nikaḷaṅka-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of
Śāttappāḍi in Vēśālippāḍi[nāḍu*]. 96 sheep and a ram are said to have been
purchased by the former with this amount for the daily supply of ghee.

edition.l: muvāpperāṭu 96- m takar 1- m koṇṭu nicatam uḻa-

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of ten kāśu to provide for (an annual) supply of a pair of cloths
with the interest thereon, to the image of Kūttapperumāḷ (Naṭaraja) ‘who is pleased
to dance on the south side of the stone temple’, by Araiyaṉ Gēyaviḍaṅgaṉ of the
Dānatoṅga-teriñja-Kaikkōḷar (regiment). The donor is stated to have also built shrines
to gods Kūttar, Gaṇapatiyār and Pichchadēvar (Bhikshāṭana) in the temple.
Dānatuṅga was a title of Parāntaka I (cf. No. 7 above).

l.ta: [m pariya]ṭṭattukku vaitta kācu pattu ippattuk [kāciṉ] pali-
ṉom tevar paṇṭārattey aṉṟu ci[kāriya]ñ ce-

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for a lamp in the temple, left in
charge of the mahā-sabhā of the village, by Echchil Śadurākaraṉ alias Teṉṉavaṉ
Miṟaināṭṭu-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Dēvaṉguḍi in Miṟaikkūṟṟam. The details of the
date given viz., Tulā, Sunday, Jyēshṭhā, which would correspond to A.D. 969,
October 17, makes the inscription assignable to Uttama-Chōḷa.

l.ta: mikku vaṭakarai miṟaikūṟṟattu tevaṉkuṭi tevaṅkuṭaiyāṉ eccil[c] ca-

edition.l: eriya vaiyt[ta] tirunontāviḷakku 1- kku vaiytta poṉ 125 paṉṉiru-

l.ta: mai peṟṟa tirukkeṭṭai mutal oru nontāviḷakku erippatāka vaiytta itu

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1920.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is probably an inscription of Ariñjaya like Nos. 11 and 12 above. This
records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple on
behalf of Āchchaṉ Baladēvan (perhaps deceased) by members of the regiment
Parāntaka-teriñja-Kaikkōḷar.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold with which to by 96 sheep
and a ram for burning a lamp in the temple, by Araiyaṉ Vēṅgaḍavaṉ of the
Mūttavāḻpeṟṟa-Kaikkōḷar (regiment). The significance of this term is not clear.
Probably the regiment was so named for the experience in swordsmanship of its
members.

l.ta:
vāḻ
peṟṟa kaikkoḷaril araiyaṉ veṅkaṭavaṉ kuṭut-

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1920.)
ON THE NORTH, WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for burning a lamp before the
image of Chandraśēkhara, by another member of the same regiment by name Dēvaḍi
Vidyāparaṉ.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [|*] koparakēsarivarmmarkku yāṇṭu 2 āvatu śrīviranārāyaṇaccaturvvēdi-

1- m koṇṭu nicatam uḻakku ney yaṭṭuvomāṉo mivvūr maṉṟāṭikkalaṉaiyom ||

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 586 of 1920.)
ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is another gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for a lamp by Dvēdaigōmapurattu
Vāmana-Kramavittaṉ alias Tennavaṉ-Brahmamahārājaṉ of Rājamalla-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam a brahmadēya in Veḷā-nāḍu. This village is different from Brahma-
dēśam in the North Arcot district which was also known by the same name, as
the latter was situated in Dāmar-kōṭṭam. Rājamalla must have been a possible
of Āditya I or Parantaka I.

l.ta:
vat nicata muḻakku ney yaṭṭuvomāṉo mivvūr maṉṟāṭi
kalaṉaiyom itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 590 of 1920.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
A similar gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju for a lamp by Baladēvaṉ Paṉaiyaṉ a member of the
Siṅgaḷāntaka-teriñja-Kaikkōḷar regiment on behalf of Balaḍēvaṉ (mentioned in No. 17
above ?) This is incised in ornate script.

l.ta: ivvūr maṉṟāṭikalaṉaiyom nicata muḻakku ney aṭṭuvomāṉom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 594 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 42(1/2) kaḻañju of gold (tulaippoṉ) deposited with the gaṇap-
perumakkaḷ of the year, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple and for the
daily mid-day offering to the god, with which a Bhaṭṭa well-versed in
the Vēda was to be fed able. The offering comprised rice, kummāyam
and three other dishes, ghee, curd, sugar, plantains and betel-nut and leaves.
The donor's name is given as Kōyil-Peṟṟāḷ alias Guṇavan-Mādēviyār daughter of
Bhūpālaśēkhariyār, on whose behalf the king (dēvar ?) is said to have paid the
It is perhaps to be inferred that she was his concubine.

l.ta: ṟi ñālum ney āḻākkum tayir nāḻi-

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 609 of 1920.)
ON THE WALL OF THE SOUTH VERANDAH IN THE PRĀKĀRA, BEHIND
THE IMAGE OF GAJA-LAKSHMĪ IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju for burning a lamp inside the tiruvuṇṇāḻigai of
the temple by Paḻavēṭṭaraiyaṉ Kōdaṇḍaṉ Tappildarmaṉ. The identity of this chief with
Tappildarma-Pallavaraiyaṉ figuring in two records of Gaṇḍarāditya has been surmised
already (S.I.I. Vol. XIII, Intr. p. IV). The inscription may be one of Ariñjaya.

edition.l: ka vaiytta viḷakku+ 1- kkup poṉ paṉṉi-

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1923.)
KŌYIL-TĒVARĀYANPĒṬṬAI NEAR PAṆḌĀRAVĀḌAI, PAPANASAM TALUK, TANJAVUR
DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MATSYAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kāśu for maintaining a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Tiruchchēlūr-Mādēvar at Rājakēsari-chaturvēdimangalam a brahmadēya on the
southern bank of the river, by Neḍun[dug]gai Daiśam wife of Tattaṉ Śīlaṉ a
merchant of Tribhuvanamādēvi-pēraṅgāḍi at Tañjāvūr. Tribhuvana-Mahādevi was
a queen of Rājarāja I and the mother of Rājēndra-Chōḷa I. This inscription may be
attributed to the latter.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1925.)
TIRUVĀḌUTURAI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, GŌMUKTĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the sale of 4 mā of tax-free land by the sabhā of Śāttanūr
a brahmadēya of Tiraimūr-nāḍu on the southern bank, to Śaṅkayaṉ Oṟṟi a member of
the regiment Ādityapanma-terinda-Kaikkōḷar for maintaining a coconut garden (o 1000
trees) belonging to the temple of Tiruvāvaḍutuṟai-Mahādēva and for growink a
flower-garden. The yield of 100 palms is said to have been his share for the former
work. This might be assigned to Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1925.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the ends of lines. It records a similar sale of 3 mā of land
at Kīḻ-Kumāramaṅgalam bythe same assembly, probably to a different person (name
lost) for maintaining a flower-garden out of the yield of another coconut garden,
and for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple for each of the mā of land.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 2 āvatu teṉkarai tiraimurnāṭṭu brahmateyañ

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1926.)
TIRUVIḶAKKUḌI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE MAṆAVĀḶĒŚVARA, TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The details of date correspond to A.D. 969, April 12 in the reign of Uttama-
Chōḷa. The inscription is built in at the right end. It records the remission
of taxes on some plots of land belonging to the temple at Tiruvēḻvikkuḍi by
purchase, by the assembly of Veḍēlviḍugudēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya
in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu on the northern bank of the river, for a lump-sum of 50 īḻakkāśu
received by them from a member of the Karikāla-Chōḷa-teriñja-Kaikkōḷar regiment.
This body was evidently named after Āditya II.

l.ta:
peṟṟa tiruvoṇattiṉāṉṟu tiruveḻvikkuṭi śrīkoyilile peruṅkuṟi kūṭiiruntu
paṇitta••••

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1927.)
KĀVĒRIPURAM, BHAVANI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE JALAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment in gold of 4 kaḻañju per year, a piece of wet land, a
house and a garden, made by the Vaḷañjiyar (community) of Tuvaḷai, as jīvita
for Araiyaṉ Araiyaṉ and his heirs, for playing on the drum during the śrībali service
in the temple of Nāṭṭīśvara built by Śāttaṉ Karuvūr alias Kannaradēva-Māyilaṭṭi
a resident of the same place. This might be an inscription of the Koṅgu king
Parakēsari Vikrama-Chōḷa whose inscription is also found in the same temple.

l.ta:
varkku śrīpali koṭṭu muvaccaṉ araiya ṉaraiyaṉukkum makkaḷuk
kum
vaitta jivitam āṇṭuvarai nāṟkaḻañcum muṉṟokku nilamum maṉaiyun toṭṭamum vai

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1928.)
TIRUPPAḺANAM, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ĀPATSAHĀYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is very probably an inscription of Rājēndra-Chōḷa I. It is damaged in places.
It records an endowment of 5 pieces of land at Pēraṅgālakkuḍi after purchase (from
the temple) and reclamation for feeding 4 Brāhmaṇas in the temple of Tiruppaḻanam
in Miṟai-kūṟṟam, (after the daily midday offerings), by Kaṟṟaḷi-Baṭṭālakaṉ a member
of the regiment Arumoḻidēva-teriñja Kaikkōḷar.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesaripaṉmaṟkku yāṇṭu 2 āvatu miṟaikkūṟṟattu dēvatāṉam
tiru[ppaḻaṉa]muṭaiyār koyilile nicati nālavar brāhmaṇar

l.ta: taval uṇṇa ivvūr tevatāṉam peraṅkālakkuṭi eliyūtu puṉcey tiṭal ivā[ti]

ṭaiyār arumoḻitevateriñca kaikoḷaril kaṟṟaḷi paṭṭālakaṉ vilaikoṇṭu mayakkiṉa
nilattukku kiḻpār[k*]ke[l*]lai peṟṟāṉmayakkalukkum naṅ-

kum tiṭa-
[lu]kkum tiruvaṭimayakkalukkum teṟkkum ivvicaitta perunāṉke[l*]laiyuḷ ḷakappaṭṭa nila-
viṟṟukkuṭutta puṉcey tiṭal iraṇṭu māvum i-

above:

2 —tu vallatu ivaṉu[k*]ke maṇṇilaiyāka viṟṟukuṭutta vāykkā-
3 liṉ kiḻkaraik kāṇiyum taṭipalavāy vanta nilam mu-

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1928.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of land (?) for the maintenance of five temple
drummers with 30 kalam of paddy each (per annum) by Ādittaṉ Vīmaṉ of Kumāra-
pāḍi in Ma[y*]yūr-nāḍu of Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam. This has been assigned to Ariñjaya
(M.E.R for 1928, Part II, para 3.)

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1928.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land for offerings, with 4 nāḻi of rice daily during the
ardhayāma service in the temple of Mahādēva, and for burning a perpetual lamp
before the god, by Ādittaṉ Vīmaṉ of Kumārapāḍi (mentioned above in No. 30) the
chief of Aṇḍuṟai. The donor is surmised to have been the father of Vīmaṉ
Kundavaiyār the queen of Ariñjaya (M.E.R. for 1928, Part II. para 3).

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1931.)
TIRUCHCHATTURAI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ŌDANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 300 sheep left with a local shepherd for the daily supply
of three uḻakku of ghee, for burning three (perpetual) lamps in the temple of Agastīś-
varattu-Mādēvar at Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai, by one Irāmīśvara-Bhaṭārar.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is engraved in continuation of the above inscription. It records a gift of
90 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the same temple by a certain••••Rudraśingaṉ, who
left them in charge of a local shepherd for the daily supply of an uḻakku of ghee.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 10 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the temple servants
(patipādamūlattār) by one Paramaṉ Ādichachapiḍāri of Āyirattaḷi, for burning
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēva at Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai with the daily
supply of an uḻakku of ghee as interest thereon.

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1931.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 20 kaḻañju of gold made by (the chief) Tennavaṉ Maḻa-
nāṭṭuvēḷāṉ alias Koṟṟaṉ Māṟaṉ, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple with an
uḻakku of ghee daily, and 20 cows (for ghee) for performing the sacred bath of the god
on the day of saṅkrānti in each month. The donor seems to be the same as Śembiyaṉ
Maḻanāṭṭuvēḷāṉ mentioned in an inscription attributable to Āditya I (S.I.I. Vol. XIII,
No. 313). This may be assigned to Parāntaka I.

l.ta: ñcum koṇṭu itaṉ palicaiyā[l ney mu]-

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is damaged from line 7 to 13. It records some endowment for burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple with the daily supply of an uḻakku of ghee, by
Tiruvaḍi Pūdi the headman of Kaḍalvāyil of Mīypilāṟu in Poygai-nāḍu.

l.ta: ḻān tiruvaṭi pūti nicati uḻakku neyyāl

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1904.)
TIRUGŌKARṆAM, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE.
ON THE SOUTH FACE OF THE FIRST PILLAR (FROM THE RIGHT) IN FRONT OF THE
ROCK-CUT CENTRAL SHRINE IN THE GŌKARṆĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a kaḻañju of gold for burning a lamp during the early
morning service, in the temple of Tirugōkarṇattu-Mahādēva in Kaviram (?) a division
of Valla-nāḍu by Sāma-Nāyakaṉ Kuḍaiyambaṭāraṉ of Vīraśōḻapuram (No. 39, Pud.
Ins., Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 346 of 1904.)
KUḌUMIYĀMALAI, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE ROCK-CUT CENTRAL SHRINE, LEFT OF ENTRANCE,
MĒLAIKKŌYIL TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
A gift of 7(1/2) kaḻañju (tuḷaippoṉ) of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Peṟumānaḍigaḷ at Tirunalakkuṉṟam by one Araṅgaṉ Kaḷvaṉ of
Pulivalam in Uṟaiyūr-kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 687 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, KHARAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This consists of two portions, one a prose passage and the other a verse,
both recording the renovation (probably) of the tiers of the central shrine of the
temple of Tirukkarapuram at Kāvidippākkam alias Amanināraṇa-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam, by Śennippēraraiyaṉ Kāḍamāṉ Dīraṉ of Araśūr in
Pāmbuṇi-kūṟṟam. This might be a record of Uttama-Chōḷa.

l.ta: yāṉaṉā[ki]ya ceṉṉipperaraiyaṉ kāṭamāṉ tiraṉ putukkuppaṭai yi[ṭi]••••
yi[cu]vara irakṣai itu kāttār pātatūḷi eṉṟalaimelaṉa
peruku tiṟal ceṉṉipperaraiyaṉ tira[ṉ*] araiceyppirāṉ aḻakar-
c ceyta karapurattu maṉṉakkar [a]kku [vaṭaippaṭai] mātevar eṉṉak

edition.l: kumpiṭar The surface containing lines 5—7 has been scratched throughout and erased.—

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1906.)
PĒRAṄGIYŪR, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged and the sense is obscure in places. The date looks like 13
also. This seems to record an endowment of two plots of land, after their purchase
of tax-free from the sabhā of the village [Pēriṅgūr] by Rudra-Divākaraṉ a resident of
Eṭṭukkūr, for the daily feeding of seven Brāhmaṇas in the temple of Mūla-
sthānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ. The management of this gift was vested with the
sabhā itself by the donor. This is possibly an inscription of the reign of Uttama-
Chōḷa.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripanma[ṟ]ku yāṇṭu [3] āvatu periṅkū•••••
yūrār kūṭṭa[ṅ]••••••• hmadēyam eṭṭukkūr urudradivākaraṉeṉ
śrīmūlasthāṉattup perumāṉaṭikaḷukku kāṭṭa••••

yil śrīmūlasthāṉatte[y] ūṭṭuvippomāṉom sabhaiyom sabhaikuṟi[ yuḷḷiruntu
eḻutiṉeṉ sa] bhai••••

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1906.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records a gift of three pieces of land made tax-free by
the ūṛāṛ at Pāḷūr, for offerings in the temple of Śrī-Mūla[sthānam uḍai]yar-
Perumānaḍigaḷ by one AppiMādēvaṉ of Parakkōvūr in Pēṟiṅgūr-nāḍu.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecari[ panmar] kku yāṇṭu 3 āva[tu] peṟiṅkūrnāṭṭup pa-

l.ta: ṭikkup pāḷūrt teṉkāl nāṉuṭaiya puḷiya•••• muṉṟu ceṟuvum
tiruvamutukkuk kuṭutte ṉappi mātevaṉeṉ i[v]ai eṉṉeḻuttu icce
ṟuvu muṉṟum iṟaiyiliyā[ka] iṟai iḻittuk••••• pāḷūr ūrom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1907.)
TIRUVIŚALŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ŚIVAYŌGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is engraved in continuation of No. 5 of 1907 (S.I.I., Vol. XIII No. 300).
which is assignable to Āditya I and is in the same script. Probably it is a record of
Parāntaka's reign, and it registers an endowment of a quarter śēy of land at
Tiruviśalūr, by Perumāṉ Sōmāśiyār a Śeṭṭi of Aḷḷūr, to provide for the sacred bath of
the god Tiruviśalūr Perumaṉaḍigaḷ.

l.ta:
perumāṉaṭika(ḷ)ḷukku tirumañcaṉappaṟṟuvataṟku aḷḷūr ceṭṭi perumāṉ comā-
ci[yā]-
r tiruvica(l)lūriṉ teṉpakkame kāl cey kuṭu[t*]tār [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1907.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is damaged at the end. It records an endowment of 10(1/2) kaḻañju of
gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruviśalūr-Perumāṉ at Amaninārāyaṇa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a dēvadāna and brahmadēya village on the northern bank of the
river, by Rājēndraśōḻa-Mūvēnda[vēḷār] of Miḻalai-kūṟṟam, probably an officer of
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1907.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This seems to register an endowment to Tiruviśalūr-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ, of 160 kuḻi
of land to the south of Pāśi-vāykkāl (channel) and north-west of Avaninārāyaṇa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, by one Māḍalaṉ Śēndaṉ a resident of the place.

l.note: These words in lines 4 and 6 are written over an erasure.
These words in lines 4 and 6 are written over an erasure.

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1907.)
TIRUVIḌAIMARUDŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAHĀLIṄGASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a silver pot weighing about 144 (kaḻañju) to the temple,
by a native of Pāṇḍi-[nāḍu].

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesaripanma[r*]kku yāṇṭu [3]āvatu ḷ 197- ṉāl uṭaiyā[r*]kku
•••• pāṇṭi••••
laippirāṉ iṭṭa veḷḷikkalacam 1 niṟai 144 itu panmāhēśvararakṣai |—

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1908.)
TIRUVIḺIMILALAI, NANNILAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE SUNDARAKUCHĀMBĀḶ SHRINE IN THE VIḶINĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a silver dish weighing 240 kaḻañju to hold the daily
śrībali offerings to God Paramēśvara-Bhaṭāra of Tiruviḻimiḻalai by Uḍaiyār Tāntōṉri.
Tanichchēvagar, by which is perhaps meant the king himself as the ‘devoted servant’
of God Tāntōṉṟi. Tiruviḻimiḻalai is said to have been a muṟṟūṭṭu-dēvadāṉa of (god)
Veṇṇāṭṭu-Perumāṉār. This is engraved immediately below No. 438 of 1908
mentioning one Arikulakēsari-Viḻupparaiyaṉ. This might be assigned to Ariñjaya,
instead of to Vijayālaya as is surmised in M.E.R. for 1909. Part II, para 35.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
VYĀGHRAPĀDĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is left incomplete. It records an endowment of land at
Vilivalam to provide for offerings, etc. and for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tiruppulippagavar at Śiṟṟiṅgūr a brahmadēya in Kurukkai-kūṟṟam a
subdivision of Milāḍu. The donor's name is lost.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripanmarkku yāṇṭu mūnṟāvatu malāṭṭuk kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu
brahmadēyam ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tiruppulipakavarkku tiruvamutupaṭi uḷḷiṭṭiṉattukku vili
valamāṉa uḷḷūrkku

l.ta:
ellai kiḻpā[r*]kku[el]lai koṭiyūr e[l*]laikkum kuṇa[malai]pāṭi e[l*]laikkum meṟkkum
tenpār[k*]ke[l*]lai keṭila[t*]tukku vaṭakkum melpār[k*]ke[l*]lai malaiyanūr e[l*]laikku
kiḻakkum vaṭapārkke[l*]lai

nāṟperellaiyu ḷakappaṭṭa ṉilam tirununtā
viḷakkoṉṟukkum tiruvamutupaṭi uḷḷiṭṭinattukkum u-

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 720 of 1909.)
ĀLAMBĀKKAM, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, KAILĀSANĀTHA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of land made by the sabhā of Dantipanma-
maṅgalam a brahmadēya on the northern bank of the river, to provide for the (daily)
procession of the god within the prākāra, in the temple of Amarēśvara-Perumāḷ of
their village. The inscription seems to be assignable to Parāntaka I.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesarivarma[r*]kku yāṇṭu 3- āvatu vaṭakarai brahma-

l.ta: ḻaikkaṭai vāyilniṉṟu kiḻakkunokkip poṉa peruvaḻikku vaṭakku ce-
ntaṉvā[y*]kku me[ṟ*]kku tevarkuḷattukkut teṟkkum matiḷukku kiḻakkum ivvi-

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1910.)
TIRUVEḶḶAṞAI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK IN FRONT OF THE JAMBUNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is possibly an inscription of Parāntaka I. It seems to record a gift of
land after purchase at Nṛipatuṅga-maṅgalam a brahmadēya village, by a private
individual for worship and offerings to the god within the main shrine as
uṇṇāḻigaippuṟam in the temple of Tiruvānaikkal-Perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

l.note: The inscription is damaged towards the end.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1911.)
KUMBHAKŌṆAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, NĀGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a quarter (vēli) of land after its purchase for 25 kaḻañju
of gold, by Pūvaṉ Kaṇṇaṉ of Neḍumpuṟaiyār in Malai-nāḍu, for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tirukkīḻkōṭṭattu-Paramasvāmi at Tirukkuḍamukkil. The
writing is similar to that of No. 96 below. This is probably assignable to
Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1916.)
TIRAKKŌL, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST FACE OF THE BOULDER CONTAINING JAINA IMAGES.
inscription.p:
This is partly damaged. It seems to record some provision including (?) a gift
of 200 sheep for the daily supply of 2 nāḻi of ghee and 10 nāḻi of oil to the Jaina
temple•• Maiśitta-Perumbaḷḷi at Śrī-Daṇḍapuram in Poṉṉūr-nāḍu a subdivision
of Veṇkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam, by one Eṟa-Nandi alias Naratoṅga-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Nelvēli in
Nelvēli-nāḍu of Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu a division of Chōḷa-nāḍu. This Nelvēli is probably the
same as one of the two villages of the name Nemmēli in the Nannilam and the
Nagapattinam talukas of the Tanjavur district.

l.ta: ttāṉ itu muṭṭāme aṭṭuvippa••• runūṟu āṭu-

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 952 of 1918.)
PAḺŪR, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, SUNDARĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the re-allotment in certain proportions, to the deities in three shrines,
of 10(1/2) mā of the old dēvadāna land belonging to the temple of Nakkar Paramēśvarar
of Paḻuvūr in Viḷattūr-nāḍu, and originally intended for the daily supply of 6 nāḻi of rice
for offerings to the god in the main shrine (tiruvuṇṇāḻigai).

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu 3- ṟāvatu viḷattūrnāṭṭup paḻuvūr nakkar koyil
parameśvarar paḻan tevatāṉam paṭi yeḻutiyatu tiruvuṇṇāḻikaippuṟam tiruvamu-

l.ta:
tukku nicati arici patteṭṭukkuttal aṟunāḻippaṭiyāka ceyta nilam teṉtirukkoyil
muṉṟumāvum itaṉ vaṭavāy iraṇṭumāvum [ittiru]kkoyil araikkālum-

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 355 of 1918.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Like the inscription No. 52 above, this also re-records (from an older
document) the ownership by the temple of three pieces of land which had been
granted to it as archanābhōga (for worship).

l.ta: laṉ nālu mākkāṇiyum itaṉ kiḻai mukkāṇiyu-

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 358 of 1918.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment of land tax-free, for the (maintenance of the) priests
performing worship in the temple of Paramēśvara at Tiruppaḻūr, by Araṅgaṉ Veṇṇāval of
Kāraikkuḍi in Viḷattūr-nāḍu. The land seems to have needed some improvement
which was left to be effected by the beneficiary.

l.ta: tiṉai ceyvārkku caṉtirātittavaṟku nilkka cey[ta] nilamāva-
ṭakkum nilimukkāṇikku kiḻakkum aṅkeya teṉ va•• ḻikaṟai-
•••• kku iṟaiyum eccoṟum iṟātitākavum veṭṭi vetiṉai

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1929.)
TIRUMAḺAVĀḌI, UDAIYARPALAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VAIDYANĀTHA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 4 mā of land at Tennūr in Kalaiyamaṅgalam a brahma
dāya and dēvadāna of Kīḻpilāṟu, a sub-division of Poygai-nāḍu, for maintaining a
petual lamp in the temple of Tirumaḻuvādi-Mahādēva, by Ariñjigai Kēṇi the
daughter of Mūtta-Uḍaiyār [the elder lord ?]. She is stated to have purchased it for
20 kaḻañju of gold including the cost of tax-exemption, while the kīḻ-mudal right
over the land had been already gifted away to the temple by one Guṇāṟṟiyaṉ
(Guṇāḍhya) T[r*]iyambakaṉ of Kalaiyamaṅgalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripanmarkku yāṇṭu 3āvatu tirumaḻuvāṭi mahādēvarkku nicatam
uḻakku ne[y*]yāl oru nontāviḷa-

l.ta:
kku erippataṟku mut[ta] uṭaiyār makaḷār aṟiñcik[ai] keṇiyen po[y*]kaināṭṭu kiḻpilāṟṟu
tevatāna

kalai[yamaṅkalat]tu tennūr nālmāceykku ellai kiḻpāṟ[k]kellai [ciṟuvaṇṭur na]tta[t*]tukku
meṟkum tenpāṟ-

kellai uḷciṟuvāykkālukku vaṭakkum melpālkellai oḻukkaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
tevarorumāvukku teṟkkum ivvicaitta perunānkellaiyilu-

m akappaṭṭa nilam ūril vāypaṭai nānkum[āc]cey uḷḷa kalam uṭaiya veḷḷāḷan aruḷan
pakaiyaṉai conṟa pon patinaiṅkaḻañcu patinaiṅkaḻañcum ūriṭu varippā-

ṭṭāl vanta iṟai eppeṟpaṭṭutukkum paliceyālk kuṭutta pon aiṅkaḻañcu aiṅkaḻañcum āka
pon irupatiṉkaḻañcum kuṭuttu cey koṇṭu kuṭutten aṟiñcikai keṇiyen itu can

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1920.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 10 kaḻañju of gold by one Vai[va]ḻippākkam-uḍaiyāṉ
Perumānandi of Vēśālippāḍi, which was left in charge of the sabhā of Kalaiyamaṅgalam
who were to measure 2 nāḻi of rice for offering to the god on the days of Tiruvādirai
(every month), and to feed five persons in the temple with this consecrated food.

l.ta:
rumānaṭikaḷukku tiruvamutu arici irunāḻi kuṭuttu anṟu tāṅkaḷ aiyvar śrīkoyi[li]ley
uṇpatāka-
vum koṇṭa pon patiṉkaḻañcu itu ca

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1920.)
ĀDIGUḌI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE DOORWAY LEADING TO THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
AṄKURĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment of land by Bālāśriyan Āṉai Pūdi and Āṉai
Śiva-Baṭṭālakaṉ two residents of Tiruvādikuḍi a brahmadēya in Kīḻ-kūṟu of Kalārk-
kūṟṟam a subdivision of Maḻa-nāḍu on the northern bank of the river, for feeding
25 Brāhmaṇas on each of the 7 days of festival in the month of Māśi in the temple
of Īśvara-Bhaṭṭāraka, and for a special offering to the god on the last day
[tiruvēṭṭaināṉṟu], when the god was taken out in procession ‘for hunting’. This is
probably an inscription of Parāntaka I.

edition.l: ic ••••• Lines 39 to 45 are engraved on the right wall.39 ñ candrādityavaṟ

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1920.)
UḌAIYARKUḌI NEAR KĀṬṬUMANNĀRKŌYIL, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ANANTĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 13(1/2) kaḻañju of gold (for the purchase of 96 sheep) deposited
with the maṉṟāḍikkalanai (assembly) including the vāriyaṉ (executive ?) of Vīra-
nārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tiruvanantīśvarattu-Paramasvāmi, by Kāṭṭukuṟi Yajña-Kramavittaṉ a resident of
Paṇḍitavatsala-chēri a quarter of the village. This might be assigned to Uttama-Chōḷa
in whose 3rd year the details Makā1ra, Thursday, Aviṭṭam corresponded to A.D. 972,
January 18, f.d.n. 86. Paṇḍitavatsala was a surname of Parāntaka I.

edition.l: svastiśrī [|*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu 3- āvatu vaṭakaraip piramate-

l.ta: mavittaṉ ivvāṭṭai makaranāyaṟṟu(nāyaṟṟu) vicāḻakkiḻamai peṟṟa aviṭṭa mu-
viḷak kiravum pakalu merivataṟku kuṭutta po-
•••• poṉ patiṉmukkaḻañcarai poṉṉuṅ koṇṭu toṇ[ṇūṟā]ṭṭāl nicati uḻakku neyya-

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is also probably a record of Uttama-Chōḷa and registers a similar
gift by Śōlaipirāṉ-Kramavittaṉ of the gōṭṭam (gōshṭha-assembly) of Paṇḍitavatsala-
chchēri, who left it in charge of the maṉṟāḍikkalanai for burning a perpetual lamp.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [|*] kopparakecaripaṉmar[k*]ku yāṇṭu muṉṟāvatu vaṭakarai brahmadēyam śrīvira-
nārāyaṇaccaturvvēdimaṅka-

kku oṉṟu [ candrā] ditavat aṭṭi tirunontāviḷak kerippataṟku śilālēkhai ceytu
kuṭuttom vāriyaṉuḷḷi-

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1920.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is incomplete. It registers an endowment of land made after purchase
ing the same from the sabhā of the eastern portion (kīḻai-kaṇḍam) of Parakēsarich-
chēri, by Mūttaṉ Kāmaṉ alias Nārā[ya*]ṇa-Viḻuppērarayaṉ for the daily offering
of a sweet dish called akkāraḍiyal to the god. The ingredients for this preparation
are given as follows: rice-4 nāḻi: dol-2 nāḻi: milk-4 nāḻi: ripe plantains-10: sugar-14
palam: and ghee-1 uḻakku.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu 3 āvatu vaṭakarai brahmadēyam śrīviranārā
yaṇaccaturvvēdimaṅgalattu tiruvanantēśvarattu bha[ṭṭā]rakarai candrādityavat niyatam
akkāraṭiyallamutu ceyvataṟku mūttaṉ kāmaṉāṉa nārāyaṇaviḻupperāyaṉ ivvūr
parakēsaricceri kiḻaikaṇ-

l.ta:
yārpakkal vilaikoṇṭu kuṭutta nilattāl nittam nāṉāḻi yariciyum parup
pirunāḻiyum pāl nāṉāḻiyum vāḻaippaḻam pattum carkkarai
l.note:
The sequence in the mention of 2 perpetual lamps followed by a reference to land is not clear
in the absence of the continuation to the record.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 602 of 1920.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp before
the image of Chandraśēkhara-Perumāḷ in the temple, by Pañchavanmādēvi the
consort (araiśi) of Araiyaṉ Mahimālayaṉ alias Parāntaka-Muttaraiyaṉ. The
maṉṟāḍikkalanai of the place received the amount and agreed to supply an uḻakku
of ghee daily for the purpose. This is probably an inscription of Uttama-Chōḷa.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟkku yāṇṭu 3- āvatu vaṭakarai brahmadēyam śrīviranārāyaṇa-
ccaturvvēdimaṅgalattu tiruvanantēśvarattu candraśēkharapperumāḷukku araiyaṉ mahimālayaṉāṉa
[ parānta] kamuttaraiyaṉ araici[yāṉa] pañcavaṉmātevi ca-

l.ta:
ippoṉ paṉṉirukaḻañ
caraiyum koṇṭu ivvūr maṉṟāṭikkalaṉaiyom ni

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 605 of 1920.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is incomplete. It records an allotment of lands belonging to the temple,
among the members of the establishment in certain proportions according to the
emoluments they were till now receiving (in cash), made by Kaṇḍiyūr Dēvaṉ-Dēvaṉ
of Bhuvanachūḷāmaṇichchēri the managing officer of the temple (śrīkāryam
tiruttugiṉṟa), on an order received from three madhyasthas representing the wards
Tribhuvanamahādēvichchēri, Kōdaṇḍarāmachchēri and Vīraśrīkāmukachchēri in the
assembly of 120 members holding office of the grāma-kāryam for the year beginning
with the Ṛishabha month. This transaction was also ordered to be engraved on
the walls of the śrīvimāna. To the names of two madhyasthas is added the
expression Peruṅkāvidi indicative of some title which seems to have carried with
it the grant of a free-hold land, as seen in the word kāvidikkāṇi found in No. 63
below. This is probably an inscription of Uttama-Chōḷa.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesarivarmmarkku yāṇṭu 3- āvatu vaṭakarai brahmadēyam śrīviranārā
yaṇaccaturvvēdimaṅgalattu tiruvanantēśvarattu paramasvāmi śrīkāryyantiruttukiṉṟa • bhuvana-
cūḷāmaṇicceri kaṇṭiyūr tevaṉ [t*]ev(ā)ṉeṉ eṉṉai ivvāṇṭu ṛṣabhanāyaṟu-
mutal samvatsaram grāmakāryyantiruttukiṉṟa 120var perumakkaḷi[l*] paramasvāmiyai
abhiṣēkañ ceyvārkkum paricarikkum māṇikaḷukkum istānamuṭaiya śivabrāhmaṇar-
kkum śrībalikoṭṭu muvaccarkkum tirumeykāppārkkun tiruvalakiṭṭu tirumeḻukkiṭuvārkkum
tirunantavāṉa miṟaikkuṅ kuṭikaḷukkum maṟṟu mepperppaṭṭārum vevveṟu vaccaṟpaṭi peṟa
uṭaiyārkku avvavar peṟum paṭikkut takkavāṟu dēvaruṭaiya nilaṅkaḷile-

y tribhuvanamahādēviccer madhyasthan tiruveḷḷaṟaip peruṅkāvitiyum śrīkodaṇḍarāmacceri
madhyasthan vāṉavaṉmahādēvip peruṅ(ruṅ)kāvitiyum eḻuttiṉāl nilanivantamaṭaittu
candrādityavat niṟpataṟku śilālēkhaiyāka śrīvimānattiley veṭṭuvittuk kāṇi
ceytukuṭukkaveṉṟu viraśrīkāmukacceri madhyasthan alaṅkārottamaṉākiya kūttaṉeḻut
tiṉāl śrīmukantara nāṉiśrīmukappaṭi tiruveḷḷaṟaip peruṅkāvitiyaiyum vāṉavaṉ
mātevip peruṅkāvitiyaiyum vaiyttivarkaḷ eḻuttiṉāl dēvar nilaṅkaḷil nilanivanta
maṭaittuk kāṇiyākak kallil veṭṭiṉapaṭi sthānamuṭaiya śivabrahmaṇar kauśikaṉ
sarvvānmaśivar makaṉ aiññūṟṟuvabhaṭṭaṉākiya jñānaśivabhaṭṭa(rā)ṉ māsamprati
uṭaiya nāḷ 7- m mivaṉ ṟampi viranāraṇapaṭṭaṉuṭaiya

nāḷ 5- m kāśyapa ṉbhṛkupri yaṉ śrīkaṇṭaṉuṭaiya nāḷ9 m kauśikaṉ kūttaṉ
centaṉuntampimārumuṭaiya nāḷ32 mbhāradvāji iḷaiyāṉ paramēśvara ṉstrīdhana-
muṭaiya nāḷ 32 m kāppiyaṉ tiruvāḷaṉ paṭṭālakaṉuṭaiya nāḷ12 māka ivarkaḷip
paricuṭaiya nāḷāliśrīkoyil kūttapperumāḷaiyumṛṣabhavāha napperumāḷaiyum mutalāka
maṟṟumuḷḷa tirumeṉikaḷaiyellāmarcci ppāṉoruvaṉu mivva[k*]ai paricarippā ṉoruvaṉum
••• taṭuvā ṉoruvaṉum••••ttu tiruvamitu kāṭṭuvā ṉoruvaṉumśrībali kku
viḷakkumdhū pamum piṭippā ṉoruvaṉum parikalaṅkaḷ māṟṟip pūcakkaṭavā ṉoruvaṉumāka
ippaṇikaḷ ceytuṭaiyā raṟuvarkku makappaṭa mutaleṟṟi nicata morottarkku
ākiccaṟpaṭi1- māka tu The continuation is probably lost. •••••

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1921.)
PUḶḶAMAṄGAI NEAR PAŚUPATIKŌYIL, PAPANASAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The details of the date given in the inscription correspond to A.D. 973,
March 13, in the reign of Uttama-Chōḷa. The record states that the mahāsabhā of
Puḷḷamaṅgalam a brahmadēya village in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam, which met together after
beat of drum in the court-yard in front of the temple of Tiruvālandurai, sold tax-free
a land 1(1/2) mā in extent, situated in Kaṇḍamaṅgalam (another brahmadēya village)
to the temple of Kāḷāpiḍāri in the centre of their village, for 25 Īḻakkāśu received
from the karaṇattār of that locality. This land is stated to have been confiscated
out of the 3 mā belonging as kāvidikkāṇi to Tiruveṇkāṭṭaḍigaḷ alias Eḻunūṟṟaim-
battunālvaṉ the madhyastha of the village and his brothers, as the former failed to
render account for the money and paddy left in his charge for disbursement
to certain Veḷḷāḷas and Brāhmaṇas.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1922.)
ARAYAPURAM (NEAR PĀPANĀŚAM), PAPANASAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
RĀMALIṄGASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is engraved in peculiar characters and the meaning is obscure. It
purports to be a document (sādanam) with a duplicate in copper-plate (?), issued by
the nagarattār and the padi assembled together, making a grant of a thousand kalam
of paddy “to this god”.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1923.)
KŌYIL-TĒVARĀYANPĒṬṬAI NEAR PAṆḌĀRAVĀḌAI, SAME TALUK.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MATSYAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is unfinished and stops with the mention of Tiruchcheyalūr-Perumā[ḷ] in
Rājakēsari-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya on the southern bank.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakeśarivarmaṟkku yāṇṭu 3- ṟāvatu teṉkarai brahmateyam
śrī rāśakecariccaturvēti-

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1923.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is very probably an inscription of Parāntaka I. It records an endowment
of 4 mā of land for the maintenance of a Brāhmana versed in the Vēdas who
was to perform worship to god Tiruchcheyalūr-Perumāḷ at Rājakēsari-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam a brahmadēya on the southern bank of the river, by one Kāri Kaḍaikuṟi-
chchi, a native of Ēṟaṅguḍi in Kīḻvēmbai-nāḍu a subdivision of Pāṇḍi-nāḍu. Among
the boundaries of the land are mentioned Śōḻachūḷāmaṇi-vāykkāl and Kōdaṇḍarāma-
vāykkāl. Kōdaṇḍarāma is a well-known surname of Āditya I (Trav. Arch. Series, III,
p, 109), and it is possible he also bore the title Śōḻachūḷāmaṇi.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1925.)
KĪḺAIYŪR, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VĒDAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to a Vishṇu temple dedicated to Tirukkadavuḍaiya
Niṉṟa-Nārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ, to which a certain Tūppil Chaṭṭikumāra-Baṭṭaṉ residing
at Śrī Vīranārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam on the north bank, is stated to have
made a gift of 5 kaḻañju of gold in the third year of the king. This grew to
8 kaḻañju in the 7th year, when the assembly of Tiruvaḻundūr took it over from
the temple and agreed to supply for offerings to the god, 2 nāḻi of ghee on
every amāvāsyā day of the month, out of the annual interest thereon of 2
mañjāḍi.

l.ta: vaṉta po[n*] iraṇṭu mañcāṭiyālum tiṅkaḷ irunā-

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1925.)
TIRUVĀḌUTURAI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, GŌMUKTĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is not correctly engraved. It is also unfinished.
It seems to record a purchase of some land from the sabhā of Śiṟupuliyūr by the
ūrār of Karainalūr in Pērāvūr-nāḍu for gift to god Kavaiyāḷvār (?).

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1925.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records an allotment of 6 mā of land to the person singing the Tiruppadiyam
(hymns), a quarter śey (i.e. 5 mā) to those supplying water for the sacred bath of the
god, and another quarter to the uvachchar (of the temple) out of the land
purchased at Śiṟṟānaichchūr by Kaṟṟaḷippichchaṉ of Tiruvāḍutuṟai. This donor
figures in a few inscriptions of Parāntaka I, in one of which dated in his 25th year
(M.E.R. No. 126 of 1925) he is stated to have built the temple. This may therefore
be assigned to a successor of Parāntaka, probably Uttama-Chōḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1928.)
TIRUPPAḺANAM, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ĀPATSAHĀYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold left in charge of the nagarattār (guild)
of Tiruppaḻanam for the maintenance of a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mahādēva with the interest thereon, by Nakkaṉ A[ri]ñjigai alias Parāntaka-Pallava[rai-
yaṉ]. This is probably a record of Uttama-Chōḷa.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu mūṉṟāvatu tiruppaḻa[ṉa*]ttu mahā-

l.ta:
vaṟku nicati••••ḻākku neyyāl••• noṉtāviḷak kerivataṟkku nakkaṉ
a[ṟi]-

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1929.)
LĀLGUḌI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE OUTER NORTH WALL OF THE AMMAṈ SHRINE, SAPTAṚISHIŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 32 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the assembly of Maṇaṟ-
kāl, by Māṟṟukaḷvi daughter of Kaḍambaṉ Māṟaṉ of Nallamaṅgalam a brahmadēya
in Iḍaiyāṟṟu-nāḍu, for maintaining with the interest thereon, a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tirunachchiyūr-Perumāḷ situated on the east side of the village.
The assembly is stated to have utilised this amount towards the exemption
of taxes on six plots of wet land and three of dry land belonging to the
temple, measuring nine mā and one mundirigai in all. The contents of this
inscription make it evident that it belonged to another temple, situated at
Maṇaṟkāl, of which the inscribed stones should have been utilised in the
construction of the shrine at this place.

l.ta: yom ivvūr kiḻai tirunacciyūr perumāḷukku iravum pakalum
ṅkalattu koyilāṉ kaṭampaṉ māṟaṉ makaḷ māṟṟukaḷvi vaitta poṉ
tāka ittevar nilam iṟaiyiḻicca nilamāvaṉ kiḻpula[t*]tu neṭuṅka-
rai araimāvum ippula[t*]te mukkāṇiyum ippula[t*]te naṭuviṟkka[rai] nāṉmā-
vum [kāṭu]pokkil araimāvum kāṭukāḷvāttu araimāvum itaṉ kiḻai arai-

ttu kuṭu[t*]tu imāṟṟukalvi vaitta nilaiviḷakkile erippatā-

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1931.)
TIRUKKŌḌIKĀVAL, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, TIRUKKŌṬĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is stated to be a copy of an old stone inscription engraved on a loose slab,
which was now consequently of no use and hence cancelled. It registers a gift of
96 sheep made by one Vēmbaṉ Kaliyōṭṭi of Pādūr in Iruṅgōḷappāḍi, during the
management of Pichchadēvar for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Mahādēva at Tirukkōḍikāval in Nallāṟṟūr-nāḍu on the northern bank of the
river.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] ituvum moru paḻaṅkaṟpaṭi [||*] kopparakecaripanmaṟkku yāṇṭu 3 āvatu
vaṭakarai nallāṟṟūrnāṭṭu tirukko-

ṭikāvil mahādēvaṟkku piccatevar ā[r]ācciyil iruṅkoḷappāṭi pātūruṭaiyāṉ [ve]
mpaṉ kaliyoṭṭi vaiytta nontāviḷakku 1- kkum vaitta āṭu toṇṇūṟ[ṟu*]āṟu

tavir[n*]tatu paṉmāyeśvara[r*] paṭaikkuṭṭa peṟṟār [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1931.)
TIRUPPŪNDURUTTI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, PUSHPAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records a gift of 25 kaḻañju of gold for burning
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēva at Tirutturutti by Vānavaṉ Pēraraiyaṉ
alias Koṟṟaṉ Aru[moḻi] a resident of Āmaṇśāḍu of Mīykūṟu. A garden land seems to
have been purchased out of this amount.

l.ta: ••••• ṉ ṟeruvil periya t[o]ṭṭattil me[r*]kkaṭaiya [ni]cati uḻakku ne[y*]kku

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1931.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple to
be maintained by the sabhā of Tirutturutti, by Muḷḷūr-Naṅgaiyār the mother of the
Chōḷa queen (Sōḻamādēviyār) the consort of Parakēsarivarman. This donor is
already known from Nos. 45 and 125 of 1895 (See Instroduction.)

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakesariparmmaṟku yāṇṭu 3 āvatu ārkkāṭṭuk kūṟṟattut teva
tāṉan tirut-

viyār tāyār muḷḷūr naṅkaiyār vaitta poṉ muppatiṉkaḻañcu ippoṉṉiṉ palicai
yāl

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1931.)
TIRUCHCHATTURAI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ŌDANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple and
also of a gold-handled fly-whisk for waving before the god, by Parāntaka-Pallavadiya-
raiyaṉ. Mention is also made of a purchase of 6(1/2) mā of land by the donor (for
gift), probably in addition to the amount donated.

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1931.)
ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is built in at the right end. It records a gift of 5 poṉ to
provide for offerings on certain festival days in the temple of Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai-
Āḷvār by [Ū]raṉ Pullā••• a Vellāḷa resident of the village.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟkku yāṇṭu 3 tiruccoṟṟuttuṟai āḷvārkku
ivūr veḷḷā[ḷa*]ṉ [ū]raṉ pullāṟ••••• m nāṉṟu maṇavāḷatoḻa raṭi
va[ḷa]tta tiru[nāḷu]kku•••••••••

tiruvamitu kā••••••••• paṉ

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1931.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mahādēva at Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam by Muḷḷūr-Naṅgaiyār the mother of
Śōḻamahādēvīyār. The same donor has figured in No. 74 above from Tiruppūndurutti.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] [kopara]kecarivaṉma[r*]kku y[ā]ṇṭu mūṉṟāvatu kiḻāṟkūṟṟattu tiru
[c*]coṟṟu[t*]tuṟai mahādēvaṟkku

viyār tāyār muḷḷūr naṅkaiyār vaitta poṉ

30 [ippoṉ] muppatiṉkaḻañcum koṇṭu itaṉ palicaiyāl muṭṭāme erippi[p*]
pomā[ṉo]m [ ivvūr sabhaiyom ||*]

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by the temple servants (patipādamūlattār) to burn
a perpetual lamp in the temple, for 25 kaḻañju of pure tested gold received by them
from Śēkkiḻāṉ Sattimalaiyaṉ alias Śōḻamuttaraiyaṉ of Kāvanur in Mēlūr-kōṭṭam.

l.ta: koṭṭattut taṉ kūṟṟu kāvaṉūr cekkiḻāṉ cattimalai-

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1933.)
PĀLŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
ON A STONE PUT UP ACROSS THE WELL IN THE PATAṄGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The top and the bottom of the stone are both built in. This records some
provision made for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēvar
Tiruppanaṅgāḍ-Uḍaiyār at Perumpālaiyūr in Perumpālaiyūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of
Ūṛṛukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam, by Kāḍavi [daughter of] Tiruppālaiyūr-Kiḻavaṉ a resident of the
village.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1935.)
TOṆḌŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON A BOULDER NEAR THE chēri OF THE VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of the villages Guṇaneṟimaṅgalam alias Vaḻuvāmoḻi-
Ārāndamaṅgalam and the hamlets Paḷḷiyuḍaiyāṉ-viḷāgam and Perumbaḷḷi-viḷāgam
together with two garden-lands with wells at Toṇḍūr, as paḷḷichchandam to the
Jaina teacher Vachchiraśiṅga-Iḷamperumānaḍigaḷ at Parambūr and his disciples
in perpetuity, by the chief Viṇṇakōvaraiyaṉ Vayiri Malaiyaṉ.

l.ta: kuk kiḻpāl vaṭapāl kiṇaṟun toṭṭamu mivaḻiyiṉ

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1935.)
ON THE SAME BOULDER.
inscription.p:
This seems to record the levy of a cess at one nāḻi on every kāḍi of paddy
grown in the village, for the proper upkeep of the local (irrigation) tank by the same
chief Viṇṇakōvaraiyaṉ Vayiri Malaiyaṉ of Viṇṇakōvaraiyar-nāḍu a sub-division of
Śiṅgapura-nāḍu, with the consent of the residents of the nāḍu.

l.ta: ṉ ivai rakṣippāṉ aṭi eṉ ṟa-

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1904.)
TIRUGŌKARṆAM, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE FOURTH PILLAR (FROM THE RIGHT) IN FRONT OF THE ROCK-CUT CENTRAL
SHRINE, GŌKARṆĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged at the end. It records a gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Gōkarṇattu-Mahādēva at Tiruvēḷpūr in
Kavira-nāḍu (?) a subdivision of Valla-nāḍu (No. 41, Pud. Ins., Texts). This donor
(name not clear) seems to have been a resident of Śiṟukuḷattūr.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPUNDI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
VĒDĀRAṆYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mahādēva at Tirumaṟaikkāḍu, by a certain Śiṟiyāṉ Kuvāvaṉ alias Uttuṅgatuṅga-
Pallavaraiyaṉ of Kōṭṭūr in Nenmali-nāḍu. The donor was probably a chief who
got his designation after the title Uttuṅgatuṅga of his overlord the king.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 689 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, KHARAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by the ēri-vāriyam (tank supervision com-
mittee) of the mahāsabhā of Kāvidippākkam alias Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅga-
lam in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam, to burn 2 perpetual lamps in the temple of Mūlasthānattu-
Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tirukkarapuram with the interest on 30 and 3/4 kaḻañju of gold
deposited with them by Śōḻakōṉ alias Arudiyarmaṉ a native of Pāṇḍi-nāḍu. This
assembly is stated have comprised other committees besides; viz. ūr-vāriyam,
udāsīna-vāriyam, kaḻani-vāriyam, the Two hundred elders, bhaṭṭas and viśishṭhas.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecarivaṟmaṟku yāṇṭu nālāvatu paṭuvūrkkoṭṭattuk kāvatippākkamākiya
amaṉi nārāyaṇaccaturvvētimaṅkalat tivvāṭṭai ūrvāriyap perumakkaḷum udāsiṉavāriyap
perumakkaḷum [e]rivāriyap perumakkaḷum kaḻanivāriyap perumakkaḷum irunūṟṟuvap peru-
makkaḷum bhaṭṭarkaḷum viśiṣṭarkaḷum uḷḷiṭṭa mahāsabhaiyom e[m*]murt tirukkara-
purattu

ttup perumāṉaṭikaḷukkup pāṇṭināṭṭu veṇpaikkuṭināṭṭu [nākappaṭṭina]c coḻa
koṉākiya [a]ṟutiyarmaṉ ca

ttaru[ḷ*]ḷaḷavum tirukkarapurattupperumāṉaṭikaḷukkuiraṇṭu

poṉ koṇṭu [im]muppatiṉkaḻañce mukkāṟp poṉ(ṉā)ṉālum vanta

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 672 of 1905.)
TĪRTHAMALAI, UTTANGARAI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK TO THE WEST OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, TĪRTHAGIRĪŚVARA
TEMPLE ON THE HILL.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It records a gift of gold for burning a perpetual
land in the temple of Kūḍal-Āḻvār by a queen of Mummuḍichchōḻa-Perumāḷ, whose
name cannot be properly made out. This is evidently an inscription of Rājēndra-
Chōḷa I.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 673 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME ROCK.
inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of gold for burning a lamp in the Kūḍal-Āḻvār
temple by Aiyyaṉ Vīramādēviyār of Pūṅguṉṟam (another) queen of Mummuḍich-
chōḻa-Perumāḷ. It is quite likely that this donor was the same as Vīramādēviyār
a queen of Rājēndra-Chōḷa. (M.E.R. No. 260 of 1915).

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1907.)
TIRUVĪŚALŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ŚIVAYŌGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at the right end. It records a gift of 180 sheep by
a private person (name lost) for burning two perpetual lamps in the temple of
[Tiruviśa]lūr-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Avaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a dēvadāna and
a brahmadēya village.

l.ta: neyyāl muṭṭāmai iraṇṭu nilaiviḷakku erivataṟku nakkaṉ••••

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1907.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of 100 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the same temple
by Nakkaṉ Aṟiñjigai alias Pirāntaka-Pallavaraiyaṉ, a chief evidently called after
Parāntaka I (Vide No. 70 above). The assembly of the village (peruṅguṟi-perumakkaḷ)
agreed to maintain this gift.

l.ta: val liravum paka(l)lum muṭṭāme oru nilaiviḷakku e-
rivata[r*]kku nakkaṉ ṉaṟiñcikai(y)ākiya pirāntakappallavaraiyaṉ kuṭutta ā-
ṭu nūṟu ivai peruṅkuṟi perumakkaḷo[mu*]m pa

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1907.)
TIRUMALAI, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON A ROCK TO THE LEFT OF THE PAINTED CAVE.
inscription.p:
This has been published in S.I.I. Vol. III, as No. 97. But the reading of lines
4-6 which has now been slightly altered by a re-examination of the record, seems to
indicate that the two donors Viṟchēvagaṉ Piḍāraṉ Būttugaṉ and Viṟchamanāyakaṉ
Śandayaṉ Āyiravaṉ were members of two different regiments of the king, called
respectively Irumaḍiśōḻa-Kannāḍaga-Kaḍuttalai and Madhurāntaka-Karunāḍaga-
Kaḍuttalai and recruited from the Karunāṭaka country, rather than that they were
natives of a place called Kaḍuttalai. These two persons made a gift of gold for
the daily offerings to Paḷḷiyāḷvār (Jaina Tīrthaṅkara) and for feeding there with a devotee
in the paḷḷi at Tirumalai near Vaigāvūr in Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu a sub-division of Palkuṉṟa-
kōṭṭam.

l.ta: c coṟu vaittār irumaṭiccoḻak kaṉṉāṭakak kaṭuttalai viṟa-
ruvoñ cantirātitta ruḷḷaḷavum niṟka vaitto mita-
ṉukku nāṟkkaḻañcu poṉṉāl vanta palicaiyyālp pa-

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1907.)
TIRUVIḌAIMARUDŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAHĀLIṄGASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a golden hand-plate weighing 3(1/4) kaḻañju
and set with precious stones worth 2 kāśu and 2 akkam, for being worn (on the
palm) by the image of Āḍalviḍaṅgadēvar (Naṭarāja) in the temple at Tiruviḍaimarudil,
made by a merchant of the place by name Kaḷari Tōṉṟi.

l.ta: [po]ṉ mukkaḻañceykāl itil eṟiṉa kallukku vilai kācu iraṇṭu akkam iraṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1907.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription has been published in S.I.I. Vol. III. This is
stated to be a record of all the gifts of gold made in earlier times for lamps to the
temple, which were now re-engraved on renovation from stones kept underground.
The inscription is however left incomplete after the 4th line. Only one such item
finds mention here viz., a gift made by Kāḍupaṭṭigaḷ Nandippōttaraiyar, obviously
Pallava Nandivarman III (and not Pallavamalla as supposed), for burning a lamp
(after his surname) Kumaramārttāṇḍa. The authorities responsible for this re-
engraving viz. the assembly of Tiraimūr, the nagarattār of Tiruviḍaimarudil, the
temple executives, the accountant and the manager are said to have met for their
deliberation in the theatre hall of the temple. This early reference to the theatre
in temples is noteworthy.

edition.p:
Published in South Indian Inscriptions.
Vol. III, No. 124.

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1907.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged and incomplete. It gives a list of the presents of
gold made to the temple of Mūlasthānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ at [Tiruvi]ḍaimarudil in
Tiraimūr-nāḍu on the south bank of the river by several individual donors, each
specifying the purpose of the gift, thus; a gift of 22 kaḻañju and odd of gold made in
company with others by Madhurāntaka-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ the adhikāri administering the
śrīkāryam of the temple, which was utilised for (the purchase of) some vessels for
the sacred bath of the deity: a gift of 20 kaḻañju by a lady named Guṇavaḍigaḷ for
burning two perpetual lamps; a gift of 10 kaḻañju by one Tiruviḍaippichchi for two
day-lamps; a gift of 25 kaḻañju by Vīramādēviyār (mentioned in No. 86 above ?) for
a persetual lamp: and 20 kaḻañju by another lady named Oṟṟiyūr••••••••• for
a perpetual lamp. Items 2 to 4 were entrusted to the owners of an oil-mill of the
place, the sabhā of Tiraimūr and the nagarattār of Tiruviḍaimarudil.

l.ta:
m ārāykiṉṟa atikāri
kaḷ maturāntaka muventaveḷār[k*]kāy ittevar śrīkā

inscriptions the connection of which with the above is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1907.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to state that the temple authorities received
only 10 kāsu out of 50 promised by Śōḻakōṉāṟ for the improvement of a temple
land (?) called Irumuḍis11ōḻa-perumas11akkal for the maintenance of a lamp, and this
amount was found insufficient for the purpose. Further details are lost. Irumuḍi-
Chōḷa was a probably surname of Parāntaka I. It was also a title conferred by
Rājēndradēva on his uncle Gaṅgaikoṇḍa (S.I.I. Vol. III, No 58.)

l.ta: r coḻakoṉāriṭai viḷakku erikka koṇṭa kā-
cu aiñpatu ikkācil ivarkaḷ koṇṭa kācu

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
VYĀCHRAPĀDĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of land for mantra-pōnagam offerings to god Tirup-
pulippagavar-Dēvar in the temple at Śiṟṟiṅgūr by the Malāḍu chief Rājamahēndraṉ
Rājēndra-Śōḻaṉ. Kulōttuṅga I in whose reign this temple is said to
have been renovated of stone (M.E.R. for 1910, Part II, para. 24) was also called
Rājēndra-Chōḷa and the donor might have been known after the surname of his
over-lord.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripatma[r*]kku yāṇṭu nālāvatu malātāṉa jaṉanātavaḷanāṭṭuk
kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattu brahmadēyañ ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tiruppulippakavardēvarkku iv
vūr••••••

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1911.)
KUMBHAKŌṆAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, NĀGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The details of the date given correspond to Thursday, 22nd April, A.D. 975.
The first four lines of this inscription have been published in S.I.I., Vol.
III (No. 129), where it has been assigned to Uttama-Chōḷa. The continuation has
now been traced in situ and the full text is given below. It registers a sale of land
by the mūlaparaḍai-perumakkaḷ of Tirukkuḍamukkil in Pāmbūr-nāḍu to the
temple of Tirukkīkōṭṭattu-Paramasvāmi, for 25 kaḻañju which was the amount
endowed to the temple for a perpetual lamp by Kaṟṟaḷippirāṭṭi Aṟivāḷaṉ Amittiravalli
of Tañjāvūr, on behalf of her son Kāri Koḻambaṉ of the Gaṇḍarāditta-teriñja-Kaikkōḷa
(regiment). The land sold which was a quarter (vēli) extent, is said to have been the
abhishēka-dakshiṇai received by the assembly evidently on the occasion of the king's
coronation.

l.ta:
yūm peṟṟa makattiṉṉāṉṟu tirukkuṭamukkil mulaparaṭaip
perumak[ka*]ḷom tañcāvūrkkūṟṟattu tañcāvūrk kaṟṟaḷippirāṭṭi••

aṟivāḷa ṉamittiravalli taṉ [ma]kaṉ kaṇṭarātittat teriñ[ca*] k[ai]ykkoḷaril kāri koḻam
paṉaic cāttit tirukkiḻkkoṭṭattup para

kellai ipperumāḷ nilattukkey teṟkumāka iv••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1911.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
The alphabet seems to be early, but the inscription is carelessly engraved. It
records an endowment of 6(1/2) mā of land after purchase by Eḻuvaṉ Mādēvaṉ a
merchant of Nandipuram, for feeding a Brāhmaṇa in the temple of Tirukkīḻkōṭṭam at
Tirukkudamukkil after the daily mid-day service to the god.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1911.)
VĒḶACHCHĒRI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE ŚELLIYAMMAṈ TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land as mañjikkam by the sabhā of Veḷichchēri in Puliyūr-
kōṭṭam for the maintenance of a perpetual lamp and for offerings during the two
services in the temple of Kāḷā-Bhaṭāri. This is left incomplete.

edition.l: teṉpāṟkellai kuṭṭimañcurac caṭṭaya[ vikrama] canmanum ivaṉ tampiyum uṭai[ya]

l.ta: yiṉ vaṭakkum me[l*]pāṟkellai kāliṉ kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellaiva e

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1914.)
TIRUMAṆAÑJĒRI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, UDVĀHANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Paramasvāmi at Tirumaṇañjēri by a kitchen-maid (of the palace) at Tañjāvūr by
name Baladēvaṉ Kiḍāsānai.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1914.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a plate, a trumpet, a hanging lamp and a bell and also
an image of worship used during the śrībali offering, to the temple of Parama-
svāmi at Tirumaṇañjēri by a Śōḻiya-vāṇiyaṉ (merchant) named Muṇḍaṉ Araṅgaṉ
alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-Māyilaṭṭi. This might be an inscription of Parāntaka I, the
writing being early.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1915.)
BRAHMADĒŚAM, CHEYYAR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, CHANDRAMAUḶĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record a gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold for a
perpetual lamp, and 75 kaḻañju for some purpose the nature of which is not known,
in the temple at Rājamalla-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1915.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kaḻañju of tested gold (ūrkaṟ-chemmai) for a perpetual
lamp in the temple by Niṉṟayil Irushikēśuvaṉ of Kuṇḍina-gōtra a resident of
Vāyiṉānallūr in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koparakesaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu ṉāṉkāvatu irājamallaccaturvvētimaṅkalattu
•••••pperumāṉaṭikaḷ śrīkoyilil puliyū-

cemmai patiṉaiñkaḻañcu itaṉāl vaṉta palicaiyāl candrādityaruḷḷa[ḷa*]va••••• iruṣikeśu-
vaṉeṉ ||—

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1918.)
TIRUVEṆKĀḌU, SIRGALI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST INNER gōpura, ŚVĒTĀRAṆYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record a gift of some gold ornaments for the
image of Naṭarāja (Āḍi-aruḷugiṉṟa-dēvar) in the temple at Tiruveṇkāḍu, by a kaikkōḷa.
Below this is the latter portion of another inscription, which seems to register the
leasing out of a temple land to a resident of Nallāvūr, who was to reclaim the land
for cultivation and supply paddy to the temple every year at the rate of a kuṟuni
per day for offerings.

l.note:
Below this are the following twenty-one lines which seems to form part of
another record.

l.ta: n innilam aḻakitākak kallit tiritti mu-
m āka nellu muppattu nāṟkalane tū[ṇi]-

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. Nos. 9 of 1920.)
TIRUMAḺAVĀḌI, UDAIYARPALAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VAIDYANĀTHA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 45 sheep and one ram for burning a lamp throughout the
day-time in the temple of Mahadēva at Tirumaḻavāḍi, by one Kaṇḍaṉ Maḻapādi of
Pu[du*]kkuḍi in Kīḻśeṅgiḷi-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripanma[ṟ*]kki yāṇṭu 4- [ā]vatu kiḻceṅkiḷināṭṭu pu[tu*]kkuṭi kaṇṭan
maḻapāṭi tirumaḻavāṭi ma[h*]ādēvarkku nittam pakalnontāviḷakkunukku vaitta cāvāmuvā-
perāṭu nāṟpat-
taiñcu nā[ṟ*]pattaiñcum viccukiṭāy 1- m cantirātittaval pa[nm]āyēśvararakṣai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1921.)
TAKKŌLAM, ARKONAM TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, JALANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruvūṟal-
Mahādēva, by one Naran Tuṅgappēraraiyaṉ of Puliyūr in Vaṇḍāḻai Vēḷūr-kūṟṟam a
subdivision of Śōḻa-nāḍu. This is probably a record of Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 373 of 1924.)
MĒLAPPAḺUVŪR, UDAIYARPALAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE AGASTYĒŚVARA SHRINE,
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It seems to record a gift of 10 kaḻañju of gold for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēva of Avanigandarva-Īśvara-gṛiham in
Kuṉṟak-kūṟṟam by one Atiśūraṉ••••••• of Maḻapāḍi in [Vā]ṇagappāḍi, who had
deposited the amount with two nagarattār. The inscription also refers to a later order
(the nature of which is not known) of Aḍigaḷ Paḻavēṭṭaraiyar Kaṇḍan [Amuda]nār,
made in the 16th year of Uttamasōḻadēvar Parakēsarivarman, evidently about the
utilisation of this amount.

l.ta: muḻakku ney erikka vaitta poṉ [pa]ttu ippoṉ
[pat]tilum ivvūr iraṇṭu nakarattārum koṇṭu kaṭa[vatā]ṉa poṉ
ṇṭu palicai i ṭu v[ā*]rāniṟkka śrī uttamacoḻa[teva]r koppa

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1925.)
TIRUVIḌAIKKAḺI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
SUBRAHMAṆYA TEMPLE.
inscription.p: This is fragmentary and also damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1926.)
TIRVIḶAKKUḌI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAṆAVĀḶĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a silver plate weighing 400 kaḻañju by the ūrkkal for
keeping the offerings to god Tiruvēḻvikkuḍi-Mahādēva, by Ayyaṉ Māraśiṅgaṉ alias
Vīraśēkhara-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ of Guṇamalappāḍi in Nallāṟṟūr-nāḍu. The name of the
village is reminiscent of a vēḷvi or sacrifice having been performed at the place.

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1926.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 36 sheep for a lamp in the temple by Koṟṟaṉ Kūttaṉ alias
Śembiyaṉ Ārkkāṭṭuvēḷāṉ of Ārkkāḍu in Ārkkāṭṭu-kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1926.)
KĪḺAPPAḺUVŪR, UDAIYARPALAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, PAŚUPATIŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged, some portions having peeled off. It seems to record an
endowment for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Maravan-Īśvara-griham at Śiṟu-
Paḻuvūr by the wife of a Paḻavēṭṭaraiyar chief (names lost).

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1926.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VAṬAMŪLĒŚVARA TEMPLE IN THE
SAME VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Tiruvālanduṟai-Mahādēva at Śiṟu-Paḻuvūr in Kuṉṟa-kūṟṟam, by Nilaiyaṉ Vēmbaṉ a
resident of the village.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1928.)
TIRUPPAḺANAM, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, ĀPATSAHĀYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for a lamp in the temple of Mahādēva at
Tiruppaḻanam in Viṟai-kūṟṟam by Pāṇḍan Kaṇṭābaraṇaṉ alias Vāṉa••••••••• of
Puṟakkiḷiyur-nāḍu.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu [4] āvatu viṟaikkūṟṟattu te-
vatānam tiruppaḻaṉattu māhādēvar[ kku] oru naṉtāviḷakku [iravu]-

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1929.)
LĀLGUḌI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, SAPTARISHĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold with which were purchased 90 sheep, for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahādēva at Tiruttavattuṟai in Iḍaiyāṟṟu-nāḍu, by
Araiyaṉ Ayyāṟṟaḍigaḷ of Ovaichchēri a hamlet of Brahmapurinallūr in Maḻanāḍu on
behalf (in memery) of his parents Śūlapāṇi Ōvaraiyar and Kuvāvaṉ Tiruvaḍi. This
is probably an inscription of Parāntaka I.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇ[ṭu*] 4- lāvatu iṭaiyāṟṟunāṭṭu tiru-

tāka maḻanā-

eṅkaṇa-
ntāviḷakkiṉukku vaitta poṉ patiṉaiṅkaḻañcu ippoṉ patiṉaiṅkaḻañcuṅ kuṭuttu koṇ
ṭacāvāmuvāp perāṭuāka vaitta āṭu toṇṇūṟu ivvāṭṭāl nicata uḻakku [ney]

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1929.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of two plots of land measuring 4 mā and 3 kāṇi
respectively, by Kumārandai Kāri Ādittaṉ of Parāntakapuram in Iḍaiyāṟṟu-nāḍu for
maintaining a perpetual lamp in the temple of Īśvara-Bhaṭṭāraka at Tirutta-
vattuṟai in the same nāḍu. The former situated in Teṅgaṅkuḍi a brahmadēya in
Brahmapurinallūr in Maḻa-nāḍu, is said to have been originally purchased
from the sabhā of that village by one Kaṇḍaṉ Tiruvaruḷi of Pāaiyūr a merchant
of Irāyaripuram, and then to have passed on from his sons as strīdhanam to
Kumārandai Kāri Maḻapāḍi who in turn sold it to the donor. The other land was
also purchased by the donor (evidently from the assembly), the price paid being
20 kaḻañju for both. An equal amount made up of 13 kaḻañju received from the
donor and 7 from the temple funds, was paid to the assembly, towards the exemption
of the taxes iṟai, echchōṟu, veṭṭi, vēdinai, śennīr-veṭṭi etc.

l.ta: innāṭṭu parāntakapurattu kumārantai kāri ātittaneṉ ca
rivataṟku vaitta nilamāvatu maḻanāṭṭu piramapuri nallūrk kaṇṭattu
pāri pālaiyūruṭaiyāṉ kaṇṭan tiruvaruḷi vilaikoṇṭuṭaiyaṉā yi-
kum teṅkaṅkuṭit tevar nilattukkut teṟkum ivvicaitta
perunāṉkellaiyu ḷakappaṭṭa nilam nāṉmāvum mukkāṇikku ki-
nilattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai kāccuva nārāyaṇan kecuvaṉum moki-
liyaṉ nārāyaṇan māṟaṉum nārāyaṇan mātevaṉum nilattukku kiḻakkum va-
ṭapāṟkellai kāccuva[ṉ] cerumaṉ pāṇṭa nilattukkut teṟkum ivvicaitta
perunāṉkellaiyu ḷakappaṭṭa nila[m*] mukkāṇiyumākat taṭi iraṇṭāy nāṉkumā muk-
kāṇikkum vilaiyāka kāri ātittan kuṭutta poṉ

m iṟaiyu meccoṟum veṭṭiyum vetiṉaiyuñ cennirveṭṭiyuṭpaṭa maṟṟeppeṟap
paṭṭatuṅ kāṭ-

edition.l: tāka oṭṭik kuṭutton teṅkaṅkuṭi sabhaiyom itu panmāhēśvararirakṣai |||—

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1931.)
TIRUKKŌḌIKĀVAL, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, TIRUKKŌṬĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is slightly damaged. It registers a sale of ten śey of land
situated at Kaḷakkuḍi and belonging to the sabhā of Mahēndravarma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
to the temple at Tirukkōḍikāval after making it tax-free. The sabhā gave facilities
for irrigating the land by permitting the damming of the channel near the village
before its conflunence with the river. This is stated to be a copy of an old
document incised on a loose stone slab which, being of no further use, was destroyed.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] ituvum moru paḻaṅkalpaṭi [||*] kopparakecaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 4- kāvatu
vaṭakarai••••nāṭṭu brahmadēyam mahēntrapanmaścaturvētimaṅkala[t*]tu sabhaiyom
iṉṉāṭṭu

peṟuvatākavum i

ṭi inta śrīvimānattile eṟa veṭṭiṉamaiyil muṉṉivājagam veṭṭikkiṭanta
taṉikkallāl ubhaiyokam illāmaiyil atu tavirntatu itu panmāyēśvararakṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1931.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is also stated to be a copy from an old inscription on a slab which latter was
consequently cancelled. It records a gift of 13 kaḻañju of gold by one Paramayōgi
a resident of Maṇimaṅgalam, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Mahādēva at Tirukkōḍikāval, with the interest thereon.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1931.)
TIRUPPŪNDURUTTI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, PUSHPAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep made by Piśaṅgaṉ Koṟṟaṉ Ārkkāṭṭuvēḷāṉ a
resident of Nānāndur in Ārkkāṭṭu-kūṟṟam, to the temple of Mahādēva at
Tirutturutti, for burning a perpetual lamp in the shrine of the goddess and for
worship during a service therein called the śānti-viḻā. The sabhā of Tirutturutti made
themselves responsible for the maintenance of this service by collecting 7 nāḻi and an
uri of ghee every month from two shepherds with whom the sheep were left.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecaripanma[r*]kku yāṇṭu [nā]lāvatu āṟkkāṭṭuk kūṟṟattu teva-

rn[ā*]ṭṭuk kaḷakkuṭi maṉṟāṭi kaṇi[c*]caṉ teṟṟi ivūr maṉṟāṭiy turutti centaṉai
puṇaiyākak kuṭuttu ivvāṇṭu paṅkuṉi mutalāka koṇṭu tiṅkaḷ eḻu nā-
ḻi u[ri] neyyum [pi]rāṭṭiyār c[ā]ntiviḻāvukku mūṭṭi i[raṇṭu ku]••

m kaṇiccaṉ teṟṟiyum ivaṉukkup puṉaippaṭṭa turutti centaṉum i-
vviruvaraiyum tiṅkaḷtoṟum ta[ṇṭi] aṭṭuviccu tiruviḷakku erippippomā-

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1931.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Mahādēva at Tirutturutti by Sōmaṉ Eṭṭi of Nānāndūr. The sabhā of the
place received the amount and agreed to maintain the lamp with an uḻakku of ghee
daily.

l.ta: varkku oru ṉontāviḷak keri[va]taṟku i[ṉṉā]ṭṭu ṉāṉāntū-
[m] koṇṭu ṉiyatam uḻakku ṉeyyāla iravum pakalum oru ṉontāviḷakku can-

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1933.)
TIRUKKACHCHIYŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE PAVEMENT OF THE SECOND prākāra,
KACHCHHAPĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 2 plots of land made tax-free after purchase, by
Mūvēnda-Pallavaraiyar of Nalvayal in Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu for the daily offerings to the god
in the temple of Tiruvālakkōyil-Perumāḷ at Kachchūr in the same nāḍu, which
was a subdivision of Kalattūr-kōttam.

l.ta: tam nāḷ tiru[va*]mitukku vaicca cey i-
raṇ[ṭu ce]yu miṉ makkaḷ paṭṭiyum ivvi-

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1904.)
VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPUNDI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
VĒDĀRAṆYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Mādēvar at Tirumaṟaikkāḍu by one Mādēvaṉ. An uḻakku of ghee was to be supplied
daily for the purpose by the shepherds who received the sheep. Mention is made
of the liquid measure kāṇa-nāḻi.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 468A of 1904.)
ON THE FOURTH PILLAR IN THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF TYĀGARĀJA
SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is much worn out. It records some provision (gift of sheep) for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple by one Pālai Ēṟkuṭṭi a merchant of
Tāmaraippūṇḍi a dēvadāna in Umbaḷa-nāḍu. Tirumaṛaikkāḍu was also in the same
nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 686 of 1904.)
TIRUPPĀṞKAḌAL, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, KHARAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is built in the middle. This records an agreement by the mahā-sabhā of
Kāvidippākkam alias Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam
through their Tank Supervision Committee to burn a perpetual lamp in the local
temple of Tirukkarapurattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ and in that of Tiruvagattīśvarattu-
Perumānaḍigaḷ at Pākkam. For the former they received a piece of garden-land
endowed by one Śivapūdi Āchchanādaṉ son of Mahāsēna-Kramavittar, and for the
latter, a gift of 50 (kāśu) of pure gold from Śembiyaṉ Iḷaṅgōvēḷār Pūdi Ādittar.
This donor was evidently a Koḍumbāḷūr chieftain. Mention is made
of certain aṅgamaṇi-dravya (cash realised by sale of ornaments) which was the
property of the donor's (deceased) mother, out of which he is said to have purchased
the land endowed. An executive official servant of the sabhā called the Śiṟṟūrāṭchi-
koḷvāṉ who was appointed for the year, was to look after the maintenence of
the gift.

edition.l:

uḷḷiṭṭa mahāsabhaiyom emmur aṇaikkiḻ•••••••••
