iṉpattikaḻ tirukkoḻampar-
kkun teviyaṟkuñ ce[l]vanikaḻa nilam veṭṭuviyttā niṉṟu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 496 of 1925.)
ŚEMBIYANMAHĀDĒVI, NAGAPATINAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, KAILĀSANĀTHA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This has been left incomplete. It records an endowment of 143 and odd and 145
and odd kaḻañju of gold made respectively by Ārūraṉ Ambalattaḍigaḷār and Uṟaṭ-
ṭayaṉ Śōrabbaiyār two of the queens of Uttama-Chōḻa for the same purpose as is
mentined is Nos. 311 and 383 above, of providing for offerings to the god on the day
of Kēṭṭai in Chittirai month the birthday asterism of Śembiyan-Mahādēviyār the
mother of Uttama-Chōḷa and founder of the temple. The money was deposited with
the Śāsanabaddha-chaturvēdi-bhaṭṭat-tānapperumakkaḷ (committee), which was also
appointed by the queen mother.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kopparakesaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu 16- [āvatu] kaṇṭaṉ maturān[ta*]kadēvarāṉa
śrīuttamacoḻadēvarait tiruvayiṟuvāytta uṭaiyapirāṭṭiyār śrī pirāntakaṉmātevaṭika-

l.ta:
var koyilile ippirāṭṭiyār tirunāḷāṉa cittirait tirukkeṭṭai
nāḷ meykkāṭṭi uṇpatāka iśrīuttamacoḻa

yā-
rāṉa tirupuvaṉamarteviyār ivvūr ūrkkallāl kuṭutta poṉ

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1926.)
KĪḺAPPAḺUVŪR, UDAIYARPALAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE SHRINE,
VAṬAMŪLĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of land after bringing it under cultivation at Vēṭṭam-
guḍi a dēvadāna village, for the provision of offerings of rice, vegetables and ghee on
the day of Uttarāyaṇa-sankrānti to god Tiruvālandurai-Mahādēva in the temple, and
of parched rice, coconut and sugar to Gaṇapati on the day of Tiruvōṇam in Paṅguṉi
month, by Māṟapirāṉ Nambi the s11rīkāryam (of the king ?).

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1928.)
TIRUPPUGALŪR, NANNILAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, AGNIPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of land after purchasing it tax-free from the assem-
bly of the village, to the temple of Kayilāyam-udaiya. Mahādēva at Tripura••••
maṅgalam a brahmadēya in Kurumbū[r*]-nāḍu, by Gōpaṉ Śākappu••••a queen of
Uttama-Chōḷa. It is stated that the copper-plate document relating to this endowment
was deposited in the temple of Śrīpugalūr-Uḍaiyār.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kopparakecaripanma[r*][ kku] yāṇṭu patināṟā(ā)vatu kuṟumpū[r*]nāṭṭu brahmadēya
tripura••• ma-

l.ta: ippaṭi nilamum eppeṟppaṭṭa iṟai(y)yum iḻicci veṭṭina ceppeṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1929.)
LĀLGUḌI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
SAPTARISHĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a sale of land, 6 mā and odd in extent, free of taxes by the mahā-
sabhā of Nityavinītamaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Iḍaiyāṟṟu-nāḍu to the temple of
Īśvara-Bhaṭāra at Tiruttavattuṟai, for 30 kaḻañju of gold which had been endowed to
the temple for burning a perpetual lamp, by Śankaraṉ Kuṉṟappōḻaṉ of Puttūr in
Malai-nāḍu (mentioned in Nos. 363 and 387 above) on behalf of Kōkiḻānaḍigaḷār
the daughter of Śēramāṉāṟ (Chēra king). This evidently refers to the queen of
Parāntaka I of that name, though she is not specifically referred to as such in the
record. Nityavinītamaṅgalam which finds mention already in a record of Āditya I
(S.I.I. Vol. XIII. No. 325) should have been so named after the surname ‘Nityavinīta’
of a Pallava ruler, possibly Nandivarman III, an inscription of whom is found in the
temple (No. 120 of 1929).

l.ta: ṅ koṇṭa paricāvatu immuppatiṉ kaḻañcu poṉṉukkun tiruttavattu-
vatu talaimuṭaik kāṟceyyum mitaṉ vaṭavāy aṟumāvil iññilamey
mākkāṇi nilattukku ellaiyāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai viṟkiṉṟo nilattukku-
m īccuvaraṉ tatta ṉakkaṉ ṉilam nā[ṉ*]māvukku me[ṟ*]kkum teṉpāṟkkellai
kum vaṭapāṟkellai viṟkiṉṟon nilattukku teṟkku ivvicaiytta
perunāṉ kellaiyil akappaṭṭa nilam vanilamu(u)ṇṇila moḻiviṉṟi i-
m muppatiṉkaḻañcu poṉṉuṅ kaiyyile aṟa iṭuvittukkoṇṭu inni-
la aṟumākkāṇi nilamum iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu niroṭa-
cvacāṟum ceṉṉīr vennīr uṭpaṭa iṟai iṟātutāka
iññilañ cuṭṭi vantitu eppeṟpaṭṭa iṟaiyuṅ kāttuk kuṭuppomā-

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 503 of 1930.)
VAḶAPPŪRNĀḌU, NAMAKKAL TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
ON THE DOOR-JAMB, RIGHT OF ENTRANCE, OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
AṞAPPAḶĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment in gold of 100 kaḻañju deposited with the Ūrār of
Paruvūr, Punnam, Muttūr, Veruṇūr and some other villages, by Pirāntakan-Mādē
vaḍigaḷ alias Śembiyan-Mādēviyār mother of Uttama-Chōḻadēva and queen of Gaṇḍaṉ
Madhurāntakaṉ with the stipulation that in each month one village was to pay a
kaḻañju and quarter as interest on the amount, to the sabhā of Tiruvaṟappaḷḷi for the
conduct of worship on the day of sankramaṇa in the temple of Tiruvaṟappaḷḷi-Āḻvār
on the Kollimalai hill in Kolli-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1917.)
KUTTĀLAM, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE FIRST prākāra KUTTĀLANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is in the vaṭṭeḻuttu script. It records a gift of 26 cows for burning
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirukkuṟṟālattu-Perumāḷ by Ādittaṉ Mūvēnda-
Palla[varaiyaṉ] of Āṟṟūr in Tiruppanaṅgāṭṭu-nāḍu in Śōḻanāḍu. This is assignable to
Parāntaka I.

l.ta:
ṉmarkku yāṇṭu irupatu coḻanāṭṭu tiruppaṉaṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu
āṟṟūr araiyar••••• yāṉa araiyaṉātittaṉākiya mūventappalla]-

[varaiyaṉ] tirukkuṟṟālattu perumā[ḷu]kku oru nontāviḷakku erivataṟkku vacca cāvāmuvā
perum pa[cu] i[rupattā]ṟu ivai koṇṭu••••••• paṭṭi aṭuttaṉa [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 557 of 1922.)
TIRUTTAṄGAL, SATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE SOUTH VERANDAH OF THE prākāra,
NIṈṞA-NĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is also in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script and is assignable to Parāntaka I. It records a
gift of 31 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruvēṅgaḍanilai-Perumānaḍi-
gaḷ at Tiruttaṅgāl a dēvadāna village by Dēvaguḍi Kālaṉ Ālaiyaṉ Koṟṟaṉ-Kiḻavaṉ of
Kāḍānūr in Malli-nāḍu agreed to maintain the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Some stones are lost in the middle. This seems to consist of two records
one in continuation of the other, the second one being dated in the 20th year of the
king. They both register gifts of 90 sheep for burning 2 perpetual lamps in the
temple, each with an uḻakku of ghee supplied daily.

l.ta: nt[ā*]viḷakku uḻakku•••• leri••••••••• vāmuvāperāṭu toṇṇūṟum ko[ṇ]-
ṭu itaṉ palicaiyāl tevarnā••••••••• koyilu[k*]ke koṇṭu ceṉṟu aṭṭu-
ivvāṭu koṇṭu nicati uḻakku ney••••••••• ṭaneṉ kāṭan taḷappaniṭai koṇṭeṉ kiḻa-

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 335 of 1904.)
KUḌUMIYĀMALAI, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE ROCK-CUT SHRINE, RIGHT OF ENTRANCE,
MĒLAIKKŌYIL TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of a village Marudaṅguḍi for conducting the tiruch-
chennaḍai (service) of god Tirumūlaṭṭānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tirunalakkuṉṟam by
Madirāntakaṉ Irukkuvēḷār alias Āchchaṉ Vikramakēsari. It has been suggested in
the M.E.R. for 1908, para 90, that this donor might be the same as the chief Bhūti
Vikramakēsari the builder of the Mūvarkōyil at Koḍumbāḷūr. This is evidently an
inscription of Parāntaka I. (No. 65, Pud. Ins. Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 336 of 1904.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This gives details of the endowment (recorded in No. 413 above.) of the village
Munainariyār Marudaṅguḍi, and states that the income was to be utilised for offe-
rings and worship to Paramēśvara and for the maintenance of the yōgis performing
worship to the deity (No. 68, Pud. Ins. Texts).

l.ta:
kaḷukku maturāntaka irukkuveḷāyiṉa ātittaṉ vikkiramakecarieṉ ceyta tevatāṉam
kuṉṟiyūrnāṭṭu muṉai-

nariyār marutaṅkuṭi uṭumpoṭi āmai tavaḻntatu puṟṟun teṟṟiyum palluruviṟ payaṉmaramun
taṟitara[ku] taṭṭā[r*]pā-
ṭṭamum epperpaṭṭatu aṭaṅkalākac ceytukuṭutteṉ itaṉāl parame
[m vicam] uṭpaṭavum tiruvārātiṉai ceyyum yokiyārkku niyati nellu nilakkaṭakkālāl

mum maṉṟāṭip pirantaṉiyaṉ tevakuṭiyāka ipparicu ceytu kuṭutteṉ ātittaṉ vikkiramakecari
eṉ itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1904.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 7(1/2) kaḻañju of gold and a lamp-stand for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tirumūlaṭṭānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ by one Śeruviḍai Ariñjigai.
This is probably a record of Parāntaka I. (No. 64, Pud. Ins. Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 344 of 1904.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This also records a gift of 7(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Tirumūlaṭṭānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tirunalakkuṉṟam by Kāḍaṅgi a
resident of Pāmbaiyūr in Kuṉṟiyūr-nāḍu. This may be assigned to Parāntaka I.
(No. 66, Pud. Ins. Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1917.)
KUTTĀLAM, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE FIRST prākāra, KUTTĀLANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is a Vaṭṭeḻuttu record evidently of the time of Parāntaka I like No. 410
above. It records a gift of 26 cows for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple by
Dharma-Seṭṭi alias Śaḍaiyaṉ Kavayaṉ a member of the Maṇigrāmam (guild) at•••
llūr in Tenvāra-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kopparakecarivaṉmarkku yāṇṭu irupattoṉṟu ivvāṇṭu śrīkuṟṟālattu
perumāṉaṭikaḷukku teṉvāranāṭṭu••••

l.ta:
llūr maṇikkirāmattu tarmaceṭṭiāyiṉa caṭaiyaṅkavayaṉ vacca viḷakkoṉṟu itaṟku nicatam
uḻakku ney eriya••••

ṭutta pacu irupattāṟu ivai cāvāmuvā perā ivai va[ṟañci]ttu paravāka [pacukai]vaḻi
aṭuttaṉa ||

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 295 of 1908.)
TIRUKKAḶITTAṬṬAI, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VĒDAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 1/8 s11ēy (vēli) of land to provide for offerings during the ardha-
yāma service, to god Śrīkuḍittiṭṭai-Perumāḷ at Avaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
a dēvadāna and brahmadēya on the northern bank of the river, by Dōṇaya-Kramavittaṉ
of Koṇḍampatti (a member) of the āḷungaṇam of the village. This writing is early
and the record may be one of Parāntaka I.

l.ta: u [ku*]ṭutta nilam icce[y*]yil vaṭakkaṭaiya mu[n]ṟu mā nikki
ṭaiya araikk[ā]ṟ ceyyuk kuṭutt[e]n koṇṭampaṭṭi toṇaiyak(ki)

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1917.)
KUTTĀLAM, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE FIRST prākāra OF THE
KUTTĀLANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscription assignable to Parāntaka I. It records a
gift of 16 mā of land for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple at Tirukkuṟṟālam in
Tenvāri-nāḍu by Kaṇḍaṉ Iravi, made on the day of a solar eclipse.

l.ta:
haṇam innāḷ vaitta tirunantāviḷakku oṉṟu
niyati uriy••••••••

kam vaitta pūmi ilaiñci•• cciṟaiyāl pontu veḷḷappakkālāl nirappuvaṉa muṉpe
kāṟ••••

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 341 of 1904.)
KUḌUMIYĀMALAI, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE ROCK-CUT SHRINE, LEFT OF ENTRANCE,
MĒLAIKKŌYIL TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 7 kaḻañju of gold (tuḷaippoṉ) for burning a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Tirumūlaṭṭānattu-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiyūr-
nāḍu by Vaḷavaṉ Pēraraiyaṉ a resident of Nirppaḻaṉi in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam. This might
be assigned to Parāntaka I. (No. 68, Pud. Ins. Texts).

l.ta: ḷavaṉ peraraiyaṉ āyiṉa aṟiñci[kai]paṭāraṉ vaicca no-

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1904.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 7 kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiyūr-nāḍu by (a lady) Udaiyaṉ
Kalimadi on behalf of her (deceased ?) son Viḻuppēraiyar Vēḷāṉ Pugaḻaṉ of Maṇṇaik-
kuḍi. This might be an inscription of Parāntaka I (No. 69, Pud. Ins. Texts).

l.ta: kuṭi viḻupperaiyar veḷāṉ pukaḻaṉaic cātti tāyār u[tai]yaṉ kaṉima-

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1921.)
TIRUVADI, CUDDALORE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE ardha-maṇḍapa OF THE TIRUVĪRAṬṬĀNĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the gift of a lamp-stand and 90 sheep for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭānattu-Mahādēva at Ādhirājamaṅgalyapuram by
one Veḷḷikkēaṉ Piramāli a resident of the place. The nagarattār received the sheep
and the sabhā agreed to maintain the lamp. This is probably an inscription of
Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1917.)
KUTTĀLAM, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE FIRST prākāra, KUTTĀLANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is in vaṭṭeḻuttu script and is damaged. It records a gift of cows for burn-
ing a perpetual lamp in the temple by a lady by name Araiyampoduviyār wife of
[Nā]raṇappallavaraiyar.

l.ta:
kopparakecarivarmarkku yāṇṭu irupattu [nāṉku] ivvāṇṭu śrīkuṟṟalattu peru
mā[ṉaṭikaḷukku] miykūṟṟup po•••••••••

raṇappallavaraiyar maṇavāṭṭiyār araiyam potuviyār vaicca tirunontāviḷakku oṉṟu
itiṉukku aṭutta pacu iru••••[pa]cu i[ru]pat[ti]

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is in Vaṭṭeluttu alphabet. It is built in at the right end. It records a gift
of nine Īḻakkās11u by the ūrār of Tirukkāmañjūr in Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu for burning a
lamp with an uri of ghee every day in the temple.

l.ta:
nicatam uriy ney eriya kaḷakkuṭināṭṭut tirukkāmañcūr [ū]rār mel vaicca īḻakkācu
[o]ṉpa••••kkācināl••

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1917.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is also in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script and is much damaged. It seems to record some
provision made for burning a lamp in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 639 of 1905.)
TIRUCHCHEṄGŌḌU, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK TO THE EAST OF THE STEPS CALLED Aṟupadām paḍi ON THE HILL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 5 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the Tiruvādirai-Gaṇapperu-
makkaḷ (committee) at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu for feeding with the interest thereon five
Brāhmaṇas (on the day of Tiruvādirai every month), by Kāḍaṉ Pūdi Māṇikkam in
the name of his daughter Muṇḍaṅkōṉ.

l.ta: kaiyyil vaitta poṉ aiṅkaḻañcu ippoṉṉiṉ palicai

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 631 of 1905.)
ON THE SAME ROCK.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It seems to record an endowment made by [Ku]kāri
Sundari wife of a native of Pugaḻiyūr for feeding Brāhmaṇas on the days of dvādas11i
through the agency of Dvādas11i-Gaṇapperumakkaḷ at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1917.)
KUTTĀLAM, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE FIRST prākāra, KUTTĀLANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is assignable to Parāntaka I. It records a gift of 25 sheep for burning a
lamp with an uḻakku of ghee every day in the temple of Mahādēva at Tirukkuṟṟālam
by one Nakkan Appi Tirumūrtti. This is in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script.

l.ta:
ḷakkiṉukku nakkaṉappit ti[ru]mū[r]tti aṭutta ā[ṭu*] irupattaiñcu ivvā[ṭā]l ney
nicati uḻakku ca•••

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 630 of 1905.)
TIRUCHCHEṄGŌḌU, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK TO THE EAST OF THE STEPS KNOWN AS
Aṟupadāmpaḍi ON THE HILL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 12 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the (assembly) Paṉṉiraṇḍu-
nāṭṭu-perumakkaḷ of Tiruchcheṅgōḍu by Vāḍaṉ Śēndaṉ Śeruvāchchi for burning a
lamp in the temple with the interest thereon.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kopparakecaripaṉma[r*]kku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellāniṉṟa yā[ṇ*]ṭu 28-
āvatu i[vv]āṇ[ṭu]

l.ta:
tirucceṅkoṭṭup perumāṉaṭikaḷukku oru nontāvi[ḷa*]kku cantirātittaval erivatāka
tirucce[ṅ]koṭṭu-
p paṉṉiraṇṭunāṭṭu perumakkaḷ kaiyvaḻi vāṭañ centaṉ ceruvācci
tuḷaippoṉ paṉṉirukaḻaiñcu itaṉ palicai koṇṭu tiruviḷakkerip[pomāṉom]
perumakkaḷ itu kāttāṉaṭi eṉṟalaimeliṉa itu iṟakkiṉāṉ vaḻi•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 638 of 1905.)
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 3 kaḻañju of gold for feeding with its interest 2 Brāhmaṇas
on the days of dvādas11i, Tiruvādirai and Turvōṇam every month (in the temple) on
the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, through the agency of the three respective (?) Gaṇapperu-
makkaḷ of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1911.)
KUMBHAKŌṆAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
NĀGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged in places and incomplete. It is bilingual and records a gift of
20 gold coins to the temple of Tirukkīḻkōṭṭattu-Bhaṭṭāraka by Kāchchuvaṉ (Kāśya-
pan) Danapati Araṅgaṉ alias Pañchavaṉ•••• the headman of Karuvūr in Miḻalaik-
kūṟṟam. This may be assigned to Parāntaka I on account of the early writing
and the high regnal year given for the king.

l.gr: svasti śrī [||*] jvālācakra[ni]rundha santata•• pūram dū••• •
pantāmuma[yā sthit]āya tutī•• kōṭṭa dhāmnyatbhutē niṣkā[ṇā]••••••
viṁśatirmmalā tailamūlyam pra[bhuḥ] prādā dandhakaśatravē sthiramatiḥ śrīraṅganā[mā]

edition.l: yugam ||—śrī kōparakecaripanmaṟkku yāṇṭu 28- vatu ivvāṇṭai kārttikai

l.ta: karuvūr kiḻā[ṉ*] kāccuvaṉ taṉapati araṅkaṉākiya pañcavaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1914.)
TIRUVEṄGAVĀŚAL, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
VYĀGHRAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is unfinished, and seems to record some provision made in money for burning
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumēṟṟaḷi-Mahādēva at [Tiruveṅgai]vāyil in
Peruvāyil-nāḍu by a native of Kalaimaṅgalam in Kaviram (Kavira-nāḍu) a division of
Valla-nāḍu.

edition.l: •••• pparakecari paṉma[r*]kku yāṇṭu [2]8- ā[va]tu ivvāṇṭu peruvāyilnāṭṭu

l.ta:
•••• vāyil tirumeṟṟaḷi mah[ā*]te[va*][r]kku vallanāṭṭu kavirappāṟ kalaiyamaṅkalattu
ma—
•••• eṭṭi ā•• ṟai [tāḻie] cantirātittavaṟ erivatāka tirununtāviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1931.)
TIRUCHCHATTUṞAI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŌDANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged in the middle portion. It records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruchchōṟṟuttuṟai-Mahādēva with an
uḻakku [of ghee] every day to be supplied by the patipādamūlattār.

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1906.)
GRĀMAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŚIVALŌKANĀTHA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the beginning and is also incomplete. It seems to record a
gift of land by Peruntāḻan of Kīḻāṉmūr-nāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp
in the temple of [Mūla]sthānattu-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1904.)
TIRUGŌKARṆAM, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE THIRD PILLAR (from the right) IN FRONT OF THE ROCK-CUT
CENTRAL SHRINE, GŌKARṆĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The end of this inscription is lost. It seems to record a gift, by Perumāṉ Vāma-
ṉaṉ of Tiruvēṭpūr in Kavira-nāḍu a subdivision of Valla-nāḍu, of one tuḷaippoṉ
(kaḻañju) with the annual interest on which an offering was to be made to god
Gōkarṇattu-Mahādēva, on the day of Uttiram evidently in Paṅguṉi month. This is
evidently an inscription of Parāntaka I. (No. 73, Pud. Ins. Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1904.)
KUḌUMIYĀMALAI, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH AND EAST FACES OF THE NORTH PILLAR OF THE ROCK-CUT SHRINE,
MĒLAIKKŌYIL TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Parāntaka I. It records a gift of 7 kaḻañju
(tuḷaippoṉ) of gold made by Mayilai Tiṇḍan alias Avantiyakōvap-Pallavaraiyaṉ of
Pudukkuḍi in Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam, for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumū-
laṭṭānattu-Paramēśvara at Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiyūr-nāḍu (No. 70, Pud. Ins.
Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1931.)
TIRUCHCHATTURAI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŌDANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records a gift of sheep by a certain Lōkamārāyaṉ for a
perpetual lamp to be burnt in the temple with an uḻakku of ghee every day
supplied by a local shepherd.

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1917.)
SŌMŪR, KARUR TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
SŌMĒŚVARAM-UḌAIYĀR TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Parāntaka I. It records a gift of 5 kaḻañju of gold by
the standard weight) kāseṭṭāngal for burning a lamp during the day in the temple of
Mahādēva at Tirunōmbalūr by Araiyaṉ Kāmaṉ a resident of Śiṟṟūr in Mīyamaḻa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1917.)
KUTTĀLAM, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE prākāra, KUTTĀLANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is in Vaṭṭeḻuttu and records a gift of 6 buffaloes for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple at Tirukkuṟṟālam in Tenvāri-nāḍu by Kōvaḍi Koṉṟa••• a
Veḷḷāḷa resident of Vallapuram in Maḻa-nāḍu.

l.ta: maḻanāṭṭu vallapurattu veḷḷāḷaṉ kovaṭi koṉṟa••• ṉ vaitta no-
ntāviḷak koṉṟu itaṉukku aṭutta cāvāmuvāp pererumai ā-

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1907.)
ŚINNAMANŪR, PERIYAKULAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE LAKSHMINĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is dated in the 36th year of Parākēsarivarman and is engraved in the
Vaṭṭeḻuttu alphabet. It is much damaged and is also incomplete. It seems to regis-
ter some endowment for the daily supply of ghee for a lamp in the temple of Tiru-
naḍuvūr-Bhaṭārar at Arikēsarinallūr a brahmadēya in Aḷa-nāḍu by one Tiruchchēdi
Irāsaḍi on behalf of his son Irāsaḍi Śōlai. This is surmised to belong to Parāntaka I
on account of the high regnal year given for the king.

edition.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. III, No. 109.

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 635 of 1905.)
TIRUCHCHEṄGŌḌU, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK TO THE EAST OF THE STEPS CALLED
Aṟupadāmpaḍi ON THE HILL.
inscription.p:
This is a mutilated fragment of an inscription stopping with the regnal year of
the king, who might be very possibly Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1904.)
KUḌUMIYĀMALAI, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH FACE OF THE SOUTH PILLAR OF THE ROCK-CUT SHRINE,
MĒLAIKKŌYIL TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This may be assigned to the reign of Parāntaka I. It is incomplete and records some
gift (probably of gold) made by one Ariñjigai Āḻi•••• of Peruṅkūṟṟakkuḍi in
[Kū]ḍalūr-nāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumūlaṭṭānattu-
Paramēśvara a Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiyūr-nāḍu (No. 74, Pud. Ins. Texts).

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH AND EAST FACES OF THE SAME PILLAR.Nos. 443 to 471 are of doubtful dates.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 7 tuḷaippoṉ of gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple
of Perumānadigaḷ at Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiyūr-nāḍu by Poduva••••Echchiḻ
Maṇḍai a resident of Perunkūṟṟakkuḍi in the same nāḍu, who deposited the amount
with four temple servants (paṭṭuḍaiyār) for the purpose.

l.ta: r cāmi kuṉṟaṉum paṇṇi••• māṉum, in[nāl]vom

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 528 of 1905.)
TIRUVEḶḶAṞAI, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK IN FRONT OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the end of lines. It seems to record a gift of (30) kaḻañju
of gold deposited with the mūla-parishad of Tiruveḷḷarai by a local resident for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple. See No. 202 above, with which this is
identical.

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1906.)
TIRUMĀLPURAM, WALAJAPET TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 120 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi in Valla-nāḍu a division of Dāmar-kōṭṭam, by Pañcha-
vaṉ-Mahādēviyār, who is stated to be the consort of Śoḻapperumānaḍigal and to have
hailed from Kōṭṭūr in Neṉmali-nāḍu a division of Soḻa-nāḍu. Śōḻaperumānaḍigaḷ
was evidently the reigning king, and the present record is one of Uttama-Chōḷa.

l.ta: u vaicca āṭu nūṟṟuirupatu ivvāṭu cāvāmuvāp perā-

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1906.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Niṉṟaruḷiya-Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi by one Kuḍitāṅgi Ulagatāḻi of Tavusampaḻa
lai in Dāmar-kōṭṭam.

l.ta: tamum ceytār ceyta pāvattilp paṭuvā[r*] itu rakṣi-

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1907.)
TIRUVIŚALUR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŚIVAYŌGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the right end. It records a gift of 90 sheep and a lampstand
by a resident of Taḷikkaḍavūr for a perpetual lamp in the temple with the daily
supply of an uḻakku of ghee.

l.ta: tu vaṭa[karai] tevatāṉa piramateyam avaṉi nārāyaṇacaruppetimaṅka•••
vataṟku taḷikkaṭava(v)vūr iruntu vāḻum maṉṟāṭi ālamata••••

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1907.)
TIRUVIḌAIMARADŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAHĀLIṄGASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This gives the extent and boundaries of two pieces of land at Mahēndramaṅgalam
purchased (by the temple authorities) with the money endowed by prince Uttamaśīliyār
(son of Parāntaka I) for the maintenance of a lamp in the temple of Śrī-Mūlasthā-
nattu-Perumāḷ at Tiruviḍaimarudil. This is an inscription of Parāntaka I. Mention
is made of a channel called Nāṭṭuvāykkāl.

l.ta:
ṉattup
perumāḷukkup piḷḷaiyā ruttamaciliyār viḷakkukku vaitta kāciṭṭuk koṇṭa nilam

maṅkalattu pa[ṉi]ccaikkuḷattiṉ kiḻai nilam kiḻpāṟ[ke]llai k[ā]rampicceṭṭut [tiruv]eṇ
kāṭa kiramavitta ṉilattu

u meṟkun teṉpāṟkellai [k]urovit tiruvaṭikkiramavitta
nilattukku vaṭa

l ni[la*]ttu[k*]kuk kiḻpāṟkellai kāṭṭuruṭaiyā ṉilat•••• teṉpāṟkellai
kurovit tiruvaṭikaḷkiramavittar nilattu

um vaṭapāṟkellai n[ā*]ṭṭuvāykkālukkut teṟku mi•••• tta paṉṉāṉ
kellaiyilu makappaṭṭa nilam

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1907.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records a gift of 200 sheep for the maintenance of two per-
petual lamps in the temple of [Mūlaṭṭānattu-Mahādēva.]

l.ta:
vaṟku iraṇṭu nontāviḷakke••••••••• ñcaṉ vaitta
āṭu

perāṭu ivai irunūṟum iṭaiyarvaḻi yaṭuttu•••••• nontāviḷakkum ivaṉiṭṭa
nilaiviḷakkile

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 337 of 1907.)
TIRUVIŚALŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŚIVAYŌGANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the right end and is incomplete. It records a gift of 45 sheep
for burning a lamp in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1910.)
TIRUKKURUGĀVŪR, SIRGALI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
MADHUVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is fragmentary and is built in at the right end. It records a gift
of 10 mā of land for offerings and for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Mahā-
dēva at Tirukkarugāvūr by one [Śē]ndaṉ Tāḻi after purchasing the same from three
individual owners.

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1910.)
TIRUMEYJÑĀNAM, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, JÑĀNAPARAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
A few lines at the end of this inscription are lost, the portion being buried under
the ground. It registers a sale of 521 kuḻi of land tax-free by the perunguṟi-perumak-
kaḷ of the mahāsabhā including the baṭṭapperumakkaḷ of Nālūr a brahmadēya in
Śēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam for 5 kaḻañju of gold to a merchant of the village named Nāraṉaṉ
Tattan, who endowed the same after purchase to the temple of Tirumayānadēva.
Among the boundaries of this land are mentioned those belonging to god Tirup-
pāṟkaḍal-Dēvar Bhiṭāriyār (Bhaṭārī).

l.ta:
l iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟukkuṭutta eṅkaḷ nilamāvatu••• kkil viḷākattu niyāyanaṭaivāy-
kāliṉ vaṭa-

karai eṅkaḷ nilamāṉa nilam ūraṟinta perunāṉkellaiyiluṅ kiḻpāṟkellai kaviṇiyaṉ
ṟuveti nākaṉ kira-

ṉ makkaḷ nilattukkum niyāyanaṭaiyvāykkālukku meṟkun teṉpāṟkellai niyāyanaṭai
vāykkālukku vaṭakkum

ṭāriyār nilattukkut teṟkku mivvicaitta perunāṉkellaiyilu makappaṭṭa nilaṉ karuttanta
pātti
ippārtti aiññūṟṟirupattu oṉṟum uṇṇila moḻiviṉṟi varampaṭaṅkal

r[k*]ku viṟṟukkuṭuttuk koṇṭa poṉ ṟuḷai aiṅkaḻañcum tippokkuc
cempoṉ•••
kaḷom ippoṉ kuṭuttu i[nni]lam koṇṭu kuṭutteṉ ivvūr

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1911.)
TIRUVARĀMĪŚVARAM, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
RĀMANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by four servants of the temple of Tiruvirāmīś-
varam at Madanamañjari-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya village in Nenmali-nāḍu,
to maintain a perpetual lamp in the temple for a landgift of 48 kuḻi of land at Neḍu-
maṇal, and a cash payment of 10 kaḻañju of gold received by them from Ūraṉ Brahma-
Kuttaṉ a merchant residing in the Vīraśōḻaperunderuvu at Tañjāvūr. Like No. 350.
above, this may also be assigned to Uttama-Chōḷa's reign.

l.ta:
rattu tiruko••••••• makaṉ loccaṉ nampirāṉaṭikaḷ
tiruvirāmi

racekaraṉ veṇkāṭatevaṉum pāratāyaṉ••• paṉaraka cāttaṉum kaviṇiya[ṉ]
āccaṉ caṉtiracekaraṉum

varkku oru ṉontāviḷakku ku•••• m pon ivvūrk kallāl
patiṉkaḻañcum

ñciṟakil teṟkkuniṉṟum iraṇṭāñcatukkattu vaṭakkaṭaiya [patiṉā]ṟu kuḻiyum mu(ṟa)ṉṟā
ñcatukkattukku[ḻi] muppattiraṇṭum

āka kuḻi nāṟppa[t]teṭṭu ikkuḻiyāl vanta nilamum ippoṉ patiṉkaḻaiñcum nāṅkaḷ
koṇṭamaiyil innilattil
ttālum ippoṉniṉ palicaiyālum ittevarkku oru nontāviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 454.
MĀRANDAI, TENKASI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
KAILĀSANĀTHA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script and records a gift of 10 mā of land tax-free by the sabhā
of Māṟandāyanallūr a brahmadēya in Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu for offerings and worship in
connection with the procession of the god (tiruchchennaḍai) in the temple of Tirunāraṇa-
Viṇṇagar-Perumānaḍigaḷ (Nārāyaṇa). The existence of this record in a Śiva temple
is very probably due to the use of inscribed stones of a deserted Vishṇu temple in the
later construction of this temple for Śiva as recorded in an epigraph of Pāṇḍya Śrī-
vallabha (A.R. No. 309 of 1918).

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 473 of 1922.)
PĀPANĀŚAM, PAPANASAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE SOUTH WALL OF THE FIRST prākāra,
ŚRĪNIVĀSA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary inscription recording some gift of land. Mention is made
of a land belonging to one Madhurāntaka-Mū[vēnda*]vēḷāṇ among the boundaries, and
of the division Kiḻār-kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1925.)
PALLAVARĀYANPĒṬṬAI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON (DETACHED) STONES BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE GODDESS SHRINE,
SUNDARĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. It seems to record a gift of 144 sheep for burning perpe-
tual lamps in the temple with anuḻakku and half of ghee suplied daily by the recipient.
This must be a later inscription, since mention is made of Jayaṉkoṇḍa which was a
title of Rājarāja I.

l.ta:
nūṟṟunāṟpattunālu ivvāṭu nūṟṟunāṟpattunāluṅ kaikkoṇ••••• kku
uḻakkāḻākkāka vanta ney cantirātittaḷaṟ i koyilile aḷa••••
[vā]ṭu cāvāmuvāp perāṭākak kaikkoṇṭāmaikku itu maṇavāḷaṉ•••• n
yānamaikkut tirup perampala ācāriyan eḻuttu ippa••••

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1925.)
TIRUVIḌAIKKAḺI MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE maṇḍapa IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
SUBRAHMAṆYA TEMPLE.
inscription.p: This is fragmentary. It mentions Tiruvi[ḍaikka]ḻi a dēvadāna in Kuṟumbūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 489 of 1925.)
ŚEMBIYANMAHĀDĒVI, NAGAPATTINAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, KAILĀSANĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of Uttama-Chōḷa. It records an endowment, left with the
Chaturvēdi-bhaṭṭas of Śembiyanmahādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Aḷa-nāḍu, of 158
kaḻañju of gold with the interest on which offerings were to be made to god Kailāsa-
muḍaiya-Mahādēva on the day of Kēṭṭai in Chittirai month of every year, being the
natal star of Parāntakaṉ-Mādēvaḍigaḷ alias Śembiyaṉmahādēviyār, the queen mother
of Uttama-Chōḷa. The donor whose name is not clear in the inscription, seems to have
been a queen of Uttama-Chōḷa himself and to have hailed from Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu.

edition.l:
[sva]stī śrī ||— koppara[ kecarivarmma] [ṟ*]kku••••• tu kaṇṭa[n matu]rāntakadēvarāṉa śrī uttama
coḻadēvarait tiruvayiṟuvāytta uṭaiyapirāṭṭiyār śrī-

maṅkalattu caturvēdibhaṭ••••••••• śrī kayi[ lāsamuṭaiya] mahādēvar śrīkoyile
ippirāṭṭiyār tirunāḷāṉa cittirait tirukke-

l.ta:
ṭṭaiynāḷ meykkāṭṭi uṇpatāka paṅkaḷaṉāṭuṭaiyār•••• a•• peruma••••ṇappirāṭṭiyār
ivvūr ūrkallāl ku(ṭ)ṭutta poṉ

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1926.)
TIRUVIḶAKKUḌI, MAYURAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAṆAVĀḶĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This records the construction of a portion of the stone temple for Tiruvēḻvikkuḍi-
Uḍaiyār by the Vaḷañjiyar and Nānādēs11iya-Dis11aiyāyittaiññūṟṟuvar (mercantile com-
munities) who were part-residents of this place, and the completion of the same on
their behalf by one Tiruvēḻvikkuḍi-Nambi alias Tiruvaiyāṟu-Yōgiyār and his son
Tirukkaṟṟaḷip-Pittar who was the s11rīkaraṇam of the village. The Vaḷañjiyar seem to
have had their settlement originally in South Ceylon (M.E.R. 1927, II. para 46,
and A.R. No. 598 of 1926).

edition.l: —|| svastī śrī [||*] ko[ pparakecaripaṉmakku] ••••••••• ṭṭu tiruveḻvikkuṭi uṭaiyārkku

l.ta: tirukkaṟṟaḷi eṭukkiṉṟa tirukkaṟṟaḷiyil nālopāti ti[rukka]ṟṟaḷi eṭuppittār tiruveḻ-
vikkuṭiyoṭu kūṭip piriyakkaṭava vaḷañciyarum nāṉā[te]ciyat ticaiyāyirattaiññū-

kkuṭi nampiyākiya tiruvaiyāṟu yokiyārum ivar makaṉ tiruveḻvikkuṭi śrīka[ra]ṇattiruk
kaṟṟa-

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1926.)
TIRUCHCHIṞṞAMBALAM, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN A ROOM IN THE ardha-maṇḍapa,
PURĀTANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is highly damaged. It seems to record a gift of 95 sheep for a perpetual
lamp in the temple by one Nakkaṉ Āchchi on behalf of her daughter.

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1927.)
KĀVĒRIPURAM, BHAVANI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE JALAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The ends of lines are lost. The inscription seems to record an endowment of
land at the village [Tuhiri] made by the Nāṭṭavar of Vañji-nāḍu for worship in the
temple of Nāṭṭīśvara built by Śāttaṉ Karuvūr alias Kannaradēva-Māyilaṭṭi of Tuvaḷai.
This is probably an inscription of the time of the Koṅgu king Vikrama-Chōḷa (see
No. 28 above).

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1931.)
TIRUPPŪNDURUTTI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
PUSHPAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged inscription. It seems to record a gift of gold left with the sabhā
of Tirutturutti for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple with an uḻakku of ghee
every day, by Parāntakaṉ Aṟiñjigai (evidently prince Ariñjaya). This is assig-
nable to Parāntaka I.

l.ta: •••• taval iravum paka[lu]m erippomāy ippoṉ koṇṭom ti-

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the right end. It records a gift of 25 kaḻañju for burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple by one Vaḷḷuvan Nam•••• of the Vēḷar-paḍai, who
was a native of Kuśakkuḍi in Eyil-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1931.)
TIRUCHCHATTURAI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŌDANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the begining of each line and is also damaged. It seems to
record a gift of money made by a lady for (special) offerings and worship in the
temple on the day of Viśākha in Vaiśākha month. It gives also a list of some gold
ornaments set with precious stones presented by the same donor, including a gold-
handled fly-whisk and a silver salver.

l.ta: ••••• ṉ ivaḷe kuṭutta veṇcāmarai oṉṟu p[o]n rū veḷḷi

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 25 kaḻañju of gold by Nakkan Śokkai-Perumāḷ a lady resi-
dent of [Kaṟ]pagadānipuram, for the maintenance by the temple servants of a perpe-
tual lamp in the temple with the interest thereon.

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is highly damaged. It seems to record a gift of gold for a perpetual lamp in
the temple by a merchant of Āyirattaḷi by name Paḻaiyāṟan Śōḻiya[varai]yaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1931.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary inscription recording an endowment for the daily supply of
an uḻakku of ghee for a perpetual lamp in the temple.

l.ta:
cati uḻa[k*]ku ne[y*]yāl loru non[tā]••••••••• rāṉa kiḻavaṉ nilaṉ••••
•••••

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1931.)
TIRUCHCHATTURAI, TANJAVUR TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE,
ŌDANAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is highly damaged. It seems to record some gift to provide for the daily
supply of an uḻakku and piḍi of oil for a perpetual lamp in the temple, by one Śaṅka-
raṉ Kampaya••••

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1931.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is left incomplete, and the ends of lines are also built in. This records a gift
of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple with the daily supply of an uḻakku of
ghee, by a certain •••• laṉ Ka[yi]ṟṟaḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1931.)
TIRUVŌṆAM, PATTUKKOTTAI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
ON THE RIGHT DOOR-JAMB OF THE SAUNDARA-NĀYAKI SHRINE,
TIRUMĒNINĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is built in on both sides. It seems to record a gift of money for
offerings (to the goddess) during a seven-day festival in the month of Chittirai.

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1931.)
TIRUNĀRĀYANAPURAM, MUSIRI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE VĒDANĀYAKI-TĀYĀR SHRINE.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of an inscription which seems to record an exchange of
some dēvadāna lands with those belonging to Ōlochchaṉ Gaṇavadi Vaḍugaṉ with the
permission of the Nāṭṭār and the Ūrār of Siṟṟūr.
